title: Pricing scenarios for mid-contract expansion + SLA options
preview:
From: Alex Mercer <alex@prism.health>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@prism.health
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Quick: mid-contract capacity bump + co-term?

Ben —

We need to discuss a short-term capacity increase and see what makes sense commercially. Usage on our chat assistant has spiked and we expect sustained ~2x token volume for ~6 months. We'd rather avoid a full annual renewal right now but need predictable capacity and a stronger SLA for inbound clinical traffic.

High-level asks:
- Can you outline 2–3 pricing paths for a mid-contract expansion to cover +2x over current committed capacity for 6 months?
- Options we care about: a temporary burst add-on, an annualized uplift (annualizing the uplift but co-term to our existing Jan 1 contract), or a short-term committed uplift with a modest discount.
- Can you quote SLA: targeting 99.95% availability for inference (our current requirement for production), and any availability credits if you miss that?
- Any impacts to data residency / on-prem routing (we keep some inference in our VPC)?

We'd like to review scenarios this week. Can you send a short matrix and your recommended path?

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Mercer
Head of Platform
Prism Health

----

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Mercer <alex@prism.health>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: FW: Quick: mid-contract capacity bump + co-term? — need pricing scenarios (PRISM-452)

Team — looping you in. Prism (high-growth healthcare customer) has a sudden ~2x usage need for ~6 months. They want 2–3 clean options we can put in front of procurement: temporary burst, annualize and co-term, or short-term commit uplift. Also asking about 99.95% SLA and credits.

Please draft:
- A short pricing matrix (per-token economics, effective blended rates) for: Temporary Burst, Short Commit (6m uplift), Annualize & Co-term (amortized). Include estimated invoice phrasing (prorate & billing cadence).
- A recommended option (one-sentence rationale).
- Any legal/ops flags for SLA credit terms, data residency, or VPC routing that would affect delivery/timing.

Attaching a stub workbook: Prism_renewal_scenarios.xlsx — fill in numbers and return by EOD tomorrow.

Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

----

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Mercer <alex@prism.health>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: RE: FW: Quick: mid-contract capacity bump + co-term? — pricing scenarios attached

Ben / Mina / Alex —

I've sketched three scenarios in the attached workbook and summarized below (ballpark dollars for discussion; final numbers in workbook). Assumptions: base committed spend = $120k/month (current), target uplift = +100% (so add ~$120k/mo baseline volume), duration = 6 months.

Option 1 — Temporary burst (non-committed add-on)
- Mechanism: metered add-on capacity at 1.4x standard per-token rate for the incremental volume
- Tradeoffs: no long-term commitment; immediate flexibility; higher unit cost
- Billing: invoiced monthly as usage line item; no change to contract term
- SLA: baseline SLA unchanged (we can offer 99.9%); 99.95% requires a paid SLA bump (see SLA memo)

Option 2 — Short-term commit (6-month uplift, separate addendum)
- Mechanism: fixed uplift capacity for 6 months at a modest discount (effective blended 1.15x base rate for incremental tokens)
- Tradeoffs: predictable invoice, discount versus metered burst, not annualized so no co-term complexity
- Billing: 6-month addendum; invoice in two installments (month 1 and month 4) if needed for procurement
- SLA: can include 99.95% with a 5% surcharge on uplift line; service credits for breaches pro-rated to uplift_value

Option 3 — Annualize + co-term to Jan 1 (amortize uplift over remaining contract term)
- Mechanism: convert uplift into an annualized commitment (annual true-up) and co-term to Jan 1, 2028; effective blended uplift discount ~10% on incremental tokens compared to short-term commit
- Tradeoffs: best unit economics, requires procurement sign-off for co-term and possible contract amendment
- Billing: annualized charge split into remaining months; possible credit applied at co-term
- SLA: 99.95% available as standard for committed annualized capacity; credits defined in SLA appendix

Recommendation (short): Present Option 2 as the safe middle path for procurement speed, with Option 3 as the commercial preference if they can co-term — Option 3 saves them ~10–15% over 6 months equivalent.

I've attached SLA-credit-matrix.pdf for the credit mechanics. Mina — can you confirm deployment/ops timing for each option (especially VPC routing + on-prem constraints)?

Marissa
VP Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachment stubs: Prism_renewal_scenarios.xlsx (pricing tab), SLA-credit-matrix.pdf (details)

----

From: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Mercer <alex@prism.health>
Date: Wed, 28 Apr 2027 09:40:00 -0700
Subject: RE: FW: Quick: mid-contract capacity bump + co-term? — ops/implementation notes

Marissa / Ben / Alex —

Operational notes and risks:
- Temporary burst (Option 1): fastest to enable if they accept higher unit cost. For VPC traffic we can route through existing peering but will need to validate burst headroom on their border firewalls — expect 48–72h for a full network soak test.
- Short-term commit (Option 2): requires a short addendum and capacity reservation in our Dedicated pools. Lead time to reserve GPUs and tune batching = ~7 business days if capacity is available; if not, we may need to provision burst nodes (~10–14 days).
- Annualize + co-term (Option 3): provisioning window similar to Option 2 but Ops prefers this for predictability. Co-terming triggers a contract amendment; legal review required for SLA changes and credit language.

SLA and credits — practical note: issuing availability credits against uplift lines is standard; however, full account-level credits require adjustments to billing system and can add delay to invoice reconciliation. Recommend using uplift-line credits to keep reconciliation simple.

I'll coordinate with Platform to confirm exact lead times for their region (us-west). If Alex wants 99.95% as a baseline for production now, we'll need to sign the short-term commit with the SLA surcharge (see Marissa's note).

Mina Haddad
Head of Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

----

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Mercer <alex@prism.health>, procurement@prism.health
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 15:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick: mid-contract capacity bump + co-term? — pricing options and next steps

Alex —

Thanks for the quick heads-up. Attached are three clear options (spreadsheet) plus the SLA credit matrix. TL;DR:

1) Fastest: Temporary burst (metered add-on at ~1.4x unit rate). Good if you need zero contractual change; higher unit cost.

2) Procurement-friendly (recommended for speed): 6-month Short-term commit. Predictable invoicing, blended unit ~1.15x, we can attach 99.95% SLA for uplift with a 5% surcharge and uplift-line credits. Ops can stand this up in ~7 business days if we confirm by EOD tomorrow.

3) Best economics: Annualize & co-term to Jan 1, 2028. Best per-token cost (approx 10% better than Option 2 for the 6-month delta), but requires contract amendment and legal review.

Next steps I propose:
- Please confirm which option you want to pursue (1/2/3). If you want Option 2, confirm by EOD tomorrow and I'll send an addendum and a proposed invoice schedule (we can split into 2 installments to help your procurement).
- If you want Option 3, we'll need a short call with procurement + legal; I've included a Calendly link with my availability.

Calendly: https://calendly.com/ben-carter/30min
HubSpot deal: HS-Deal-PRISM-452

Alex — happy to jump on a quick call to walk through numbers.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

title: Urgent: Production spike — project-level quota aliasing causing 429/5xx surge
preview:
From: Anika Rao <ops@medverity.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 08:53:12 -0700
Subject: Production: sudden 5xx/429 spike — blocking traffic

Hi Ben / Laura,

We just hit a large spike of 429 and 5xx errors in our production environment starting ~08:40 PDT. Our users are seeing degraded UX and a retry storm is visibly amplifying requests. Attached is a short dump of the throttle counts and an SLO log snippet.

Impact summary:
- Window: 08:40–08:51 PDT
- Error mix: ~65% 429, 25% 5xx, remainder timeouts
- Affected endpoints: /generate, /chat/stream
- Sample request IDs: req_7f3a2, req_7f3a7, req_7f3b01

We are on the latest SDK and did not change client-side retry logic — the amplification looks like service-side rejections plus client backoff not kicking in quickly enough. Please escalate; this is affecting customer SLAs.

Attachments: prod-throttles.csv (csv, 34KB), slo-logs-snippet.txt (txt, 8KB)

Thanks,
Anika Rao
Ops Lead — Medverity


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Anika Rao <ops@medverity.com>, Carlos Jimenez <sre@medverity.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 08:58:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sudden 5xx/429 spike — blocking traffic

Thanks Anika — we’re on it. I’ve looped in our SRE lead (Vivek) and CS (Marissa).

Quick asks while we triage: can you confirm if this started immediately after your deploy window at 08:30? Also, are there any recent org/project changes (new projects, promo codes, or tier updates) in the last 24 hours?

We’ll validate quota mapping and check for any fallback misconfigurations. I’ll keep this thread as the single point of contact.

— Ben Carter
Account Exec, Redwood Inference


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 09:12:48 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sudden 5xx/429 spike — blocking traffic

Summary of initial triage (quick):
1) We’ve confirmed a sudden elevation of per-project throttle counters for multiple Medverity project IDs. The org-to-project quota mapping was recently updated in a background migration (rollback pending). Several projects were inadvertently aliased to the lower "starter" project tier instead of their expected "business" tier.
2) Client request rate did not change materially; the spike is from tighter per-project QPS + clients retrying without exponential jitter, producing a retry storm.
3) Fallback routing to the dedicated capacity pool did not trigger because the policy evaluates at org-level (not project-level alias), so it thought the org was under budget.

Immediate mitigations we’re applying now (in parallel):
- Temporarily increase project-level QPS for the affected project IDs (PATENT: applied manual uplift) — ETA 12 minutes
- Enable conservative circuit-breaker on frontend to reject retries with proper Retry-After headers (ETA 8 minutes)
- Force fallback routing rule to consider project-level exceeded signals (hotfix rolling) — ETA 20 minutes

I’ve attached a short mitigation checklist and will follow up with changes and request IDs.

— Vivek Kulkarni
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference
[attachment: mitigation-checklist.md]


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Anika Rao <ops@medverity.com>, Carlos Jimenez <sre@medverity.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 09:35:20 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sudden 5xx/429 spike — blocking traffic

Hi Anika / Carlos —

Sorry for the disruption. Per Vivek’s note: we have a mitigation plan and are executing it now. Expected near-term customer-visible improvements within 10–15 minutes as we raise project QPS and throttle retries. We’ll keep your SRE team in the loop and share request IDs for the manual uplifts.

Action plan for transparency:
- T+10m: manual QPS uplift applied to affected project IDs (we’ll send exact IDs/requests).
- T+20m: circuit-breaker + Retry-After headers to reduce retry amplification.
- T+30m: hotfix to routing rules so fallback capacity honors project signals.
- Post-incident: audit of the org->project aliasing migration and an automated regression test to prevent reoccurrence.

We are also prepared to provide account credits for the window impacted — Ben will follow up on compensation.

Laura Bennett
Support Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Carlos Jimenez <sre@medverity.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 10:02:41 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sudden 5xx/429 spike — blocking traffic

Laura — thanks for the quick turnaround. We pushed our deploy at 08:31 but it was a standard client change and looks unrelated. We’ve attached an expanded log dump with request IDs and timestamps, and we also captured increased client-side retries that line up with the 08:40 window.

Two clarifying notes:
- A couple of our teams saw Retry-After absent in the 429 responses which caused immediate retries.
- We appreciate the escalation; please confirm when the manual uplifts are in place.

— Carlos (on behalf of Medverity SRE)
[attachment: medverity-request-dump.json (12KB)]


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Anika Rao <ops@medverity.com>, Carlos Jimenez <sre@medverity.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 12:30:02 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sudden 5xx/429 spike — blocking traffic

Quick status and next steps —
- Manual QPS uplift applied to the three affected project IDs at 09:05 PDT (request refs: uplift-3812, uplift-3813, uplift-3814). Error rate dropped ~80% within 6 minutes after the uplift and circuit-breaker changes.
- We have a hotfix merged to the routing policy that now considers project-level signals; will deploy to prod in the 1-hour maintenance window (ETA 45 minutes).
- Root cause: background aliasing migration introduced incorrect project -> tier assignments for new promo projects; these projects were mapped to "starter" tier (low QPS) instead of "business". The mismatch caused per-project quota exhaustion with org-level allowance still appearing available.

Permanent remediation plan:
1) Roll back the aliasing migration and add a safety flag to prevent automatic remapping.
2) Add a regression test that simulates concurrent promos + migration to validate per-project vs org-level policy behavior.
3) Ensure 429 responses always include Retry-After and update SDKs/Docs to recommend exponential backoff + jitter.
4) Postmortem and customer-facing summary within 48 hours; we’ll propose credits for the impacted window.

JIRA: INF-4981 opened for the full incident runbook and RCA. HubSpot ticket HS-87392 created for account communication and credits.

Ben Carter
Account Exec, Redwood Inference


On Thu, Apr 29, 2027 at 12:30 PM Anika Rao <ops@medverity.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — that matches what we saw. Please send the postmortem and credit details. The uplift fixed customer-facing errors; our teams are monitoring for residuals.
>
> — Anika

- T+10m: manual QPS uplift applied to affected project IDs (we’ll send exact IDs/requests).
- T+20m: circuit-breaker + Retry-After headers to reduce retry amplification.
- T+30m: hotfix to routing rules so fallback capacity honors project signals.
- Post-incident: audit of the org->project aliasing migration and an automated regression test to prevent reoccurrence.

We are also prepared to provide account credits for the window impacted — Ben will follow up on compensation.

Laura Bennett
Support Manager, Redwood Inference

title: Request: region-specific retention + hashed-audit-export for Maple Financial
preview:
From: Anna Lopez <anna@maplefin.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 09:13:00 -0700
Subject: Audit logs / retention controls question

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — the team liked the latency profile and the model options. Before we route this to procurement we need a clearer picture on two items that are gating our SOC/Procurement review:

1) Per-region retention controls: We need the ability to set retention policies scoped to a customer and region (e.g., EU logs retained 90 days, US logs 365 days) and to ensure logs for EU workloads never leave EU storage.

2) Audit export with PII hashing: Our auditor asks that any exported audit trail be PII-masked (SHA-256 or equivalent) and include a verifiable export record (who exported, when, what filters were applied).

Can Redwood support a scoped export with hashed PII and region scoping, or do you have a recommended workaround? This is a showstopper for our security team.

If possible, please include a rough timeline — we're aiming to close in May if technical gating is solved. I've attached the SOW draft and our security questionnaire.

Thanks,
Anna Lopez
Head of AI Ops
Maple Financial

Attachments: MAPLE_SOW_v2.pdf (application/pdf), maple_security_questionnaire.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Audit logs / retention controls question — Maple Financial (deal: DEAL-2027-0429-MAPLE)

Team — forwarding Maple's ask (see below). Quick summary: high ACV opportunity (~$1.1M ARR first year), procurement needs per-region retention + export with PII hashing and an audit trail. They want to close in May.

Can Product/Eng confirm feasibility and a minimal-scope implementation we could offer as a formal commitment? Options I see:
- Short path (2 weeks): export shim that hashes identifiable fields on export and stores export metadata in our existing audit log table; no regional enforcement (risk).
- Compliance path (6-10 weeks): retention policy per-tenant/region + region-aware export that pins storage zone and records exported columns + operator identity.

Asks:
1) Kimberly / Product: can we scope a lightweight export feature that meets Maple's auditor (hashed PII + export record) as an off-ramp while we work on true regional retention?
2) Rishi / Eng: rough estimate and dependencies (ops/storage, infra changes, Vault/KMS usage for hashing salt?).
3) Marissa / AE: I'll hold a stub commitment but don't want to promise regional isolation until we have a plan.

Proposed next step: 30-min triage tomorrow to align on what we can sign for a May close.

- Aditya

> [original message omitted]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Apr 2027 08:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit logs / retention controls question — Maple Financial

Thanks for flagging — quick thoughts from Product:

- Compliance must-haves: (a) export provenance (who/when/filters), (b) deterministic PII hashing on export, (c) documented retention TTLs per-tenant.
- We should avoid promising region pinning in the same sprint unless Eng confirms storage zoning can be enforced without heavy infra changes.

Proposal (two-track):
A) Short-deliverable (3 weeks): \"Hashed Export + Export Audit Record\" feature behind a feature flag. Exports run through a server-side transform that hashes configured fields (configurable per-tenant), writes an export manifest to audit events, and produces a zip with manifest. This mitigates the auditor ask for PII. Does not enforce storage zoning — export artifacts will live in a controlled S3 bucket.

B) Mid-term (8-12 weeks): \"Region-aware retention and export\" that integrates with per-tenant storage routing and KMS-backed regional keys. That is a larger infra project and should be scheduled against roadmap items.

If Sales wants A as a contractual commitment, we should price it as an accelerated engineering change with a scope SLT (support window + limited beta).

Kimberly
Product Manager, Compliance

(Forwarded content omitted)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 10:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit logs / retention controls question — Maple Financial

TL;DR: Feasible but with caveats and infra touchpoints.

Engineering assessment:
- Hashed Export: LOW effort (approx 6 engineer-days) if we implement it as a transformation pipeline step before packaging the export. We will need: a config mapping per-tenant for fields to hash; a deterministic hash function (we propose HMAC-SHA256 with tenant-specific salt stored in KMS); export manifest writing to our audit-events topic; and an admin UI to trigger/monitor exports.
- Region-aware retention + storage pinning: MED-HIGH effort (6-10 engineer-weeks). Requires changes to our storage routing layer, creation of regional buckets, and adding per-tenant routing policies. Also compliance validation and e2e test automation.

Dependencies/notes:
1) KMS work: we must provision tenant-scoped salts/keys — coordination with Security and Infra.
2) Ops: export artifacts live in S3; for short-term we can use our existing encrypted bucket (US) and mark artifacts with a manifest indicating region-of-origin. Not ideal for EU retention guarantees.
3) Backport risk: if we ship hashed export quickly, we must ensure future regional migration doesn't re-expose raw data.

Estimate for the short path: engineering lead + one SWE to deliver in 2 calendar weeks (buffered estimate: 10 business days) including QA and basic admin UI.

I'll block time with Infra for a quick sync.

Rishi
Engineering Lead, Serving Runtime

title: VaultPay private deployment - data lineage, keystore & retention follow-up
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Morgan <alex.morgan@vaultpay.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: VaultPay private deployment - data lineage, keystore & retention follow-up

Quick recap from today's discovery with VaultPay and clear asks we need to resolve before the pilot kickoff. Short and actionable — please add owners where noted.

Call summary (VaultPay product + security):
- They want Redwood running inside their AWS VPC (private deployment) with strict egress controls and BYOK for encryption keys.
- Primary data class: transaction events + cardholder metadata (PII). They will not allow raw card PAN in logs; tokenized values only.
- Retention constraint: max 30 days for request/response artifacts; audit logs retained longer in their SIEM but must be redacted.

Open technical questions / asks (owners):
1) Keystore & keys (owner: Karthik)
   - Can we support VaultPay-managed KMS keys (customer-managed CMKs in their account) plus an HSM-backed option?
   - Require confirmation on key-exchange pattern (do they want us to rotate keys or will they handle rotation via IAM?)
2) Dataflow & persistence (owner: Laura)
   - They require no persistent disk copies of PII on Redwood side; ephemeral memory only and optional in-memory KV cache with automatic TTL. Can we guarantee diskless operation?
   - Are there logs we currently keep that would contain PII (generation inputs or full responses)? We need list and redaction options.
3) Network / egress controls (owner: Ben)
   - They will block all outbound traffic except to a narrow set of internal endpoints; we need a definitive IP/hostname allowlist and whether mutual TLS is required to talk to their KMS endpoints.

Next steps I promised on the call:
- Share a simple dataflow diagram and a short table that maps which artifacts Redwood persists, for how long, and where (attachment: redwood-dataflow-draft.png). (Ben)
- Karthik to confirm BYOK patterns we support and HSM options. (Karthik)
- Laura to draft a minimal logging/retention commitment that we can include as part of the pilot MSA addendum. (Laura)

Attachments: redwood-dataflow-draft.png (diagram), initial-security-asks.xlsx (spreadsheet of questions)

Thanks — this is a high priority for the pilot timeline next week.

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0191


From: Alex Morgan <alex.morgan@vaultpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: VaultPay private deployment - data lineage, keystore & retention follow-up

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. A few answers inline and a couple of clarifying asks.

Keystore & keys:
- We will require Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) via our account-level CMKs in AWS KMS. HSM-backed keys (AWS CloudHSM) are preferred for production; pilot can use CMK (customer-managed).
- Rotation: VaultPay will rotate keys on our schedule; Redwood should support CMK ARN references and not perform key material rotation.

Dataflow & persistence:
- No persistent copies of raw PII are allowed to leave our VPC. Tokenization occurs prior to sending to Redwood where possible. If Redwood needs to persist anything for performance (KV cache), it must be encrypted with our CMK and TTL-limited to 24 hours for the pilot.
- Logging: Application logs forwarded to our SIEM need to be redacted (masking for PANs). We prefer that request/response payloads are not logged to disk by default; if logs exist they must be able to be disabled.

Network / egress:
- Allowed egress: none to public internet except to our KMS endpoints (kms.us-west-2.amazonaws.com) and to our internal S3 endpoints for temporary artifacts. We will provide endpoint ARNs.
- mTLS: Yes, mutual TLS for any control-plane calls is required.

Attachments: vaultpay-security-asks.pdf (redlined answers to our initial questionnaire).

Can you confirm by EOD whether Redwood can (a) operate without persisting PII to disk, (b) support CMK ARNs only (no key material transfer), and (c) provide an allowlist for pilot endpoints + cert fingerprint for mTLS?

Thanks,
Alex
Director, Infrastructure Security
VaultPay


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Morgan <alex.morgan@vaultpay.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: VaultPay private deployment - data lineage, keystore & retention follow-up

Alex — appreciated. Quick answers and where we'll follow up with specifics: 

- (a) Disk persistence: Karthik will confirm technical feasibility, but preliminary: we can run in a diskless mode for PII by ensuring KV cache is either in-memory-only or encrypted with the customer CMK with a strict TTL. Karthik, can you confirm whether any of our runtime subsystems write caches/checkpoints to local disk by default and if we can disable that for the pilot?

- (b) CMK ARNs: yes — we support referencing customer CMK ARNs and will not request key material. We'll support IAM roles from the customer's account for decryption operations.

- (c) Allowlist + mTLS certs: I'll produce a concise allowlist for the pilot endpoints and a cert fingerprint for the control-plane cert. Laura will draft a minimal logging commitment doc.

Proposed next meeting: Thu 29 Apr 10:00 AM PT to walk the dataflow diagram and sign off on the retention table. Is that OK on your side?

--
Ben


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alex Morgan <alex.morgan@vaultpay.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 09:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: VaultPay private deployment - data lineage, keystore & retention follow-up

Ben / Alex — technical responses below. TL;DR: pilot supported with constraints; production HSM path fully supported with a short integration workstream.

Keystore / BYOK details:
- We support referencing customer CMK ARNs (AWS KMS) for envelope encryption. No customer key material is transferred to Redwood. Decryption calls are made from within the customer VPC using an IAM role you provision; we perform no persistent storage of plaintext keys.
- HSM: For production, we can integrate with customer CloudHSM cluster. That requires a brief network peer and a key-policy alignment step (we'll document).

Disk persistence & KV cache:
- Runtime defaults: the serving runtime keeps an ephemeral KV cache to accelerate autoregressive decoding; that cache is in-memory but we currently snapshot a subset of metrics to disk for crash recovery. For the pilot we can disable disk snapshots and run entirely memory-only for caches that may contain tokenized PII. We will add a pilot flag to disable snapshotting.
- If VaultPay requires encrypted snapshots, we support encrypting any persisted artifacts with the customer CMK before write; however snapshots are avoidable for pilot to meet your no-disk rule.

Logging & retention:
- Application-level request/response logging is configurable per-route. Default behavior is conservative but for this engagement we will: (1) disable request/response persistence; (2) emit only standardized telemetry (latency, error codes) to our control plane; (3) forward audit records to your supplied S3/SIEM endpoints with PII redaction/masking.
- Retention enforcement: we can enforce a 30-day TTL on any transient artifacts and will purge according to that policy; logs forwarded to VaultPay's SIEM are under your control and retention.

Network / mTLS:
- We will provide an allowlist of IP ranges and FQDNs used in the pilot. Control-plane connections can be pinned to mutual TLS; we will provide cert fingerprints and support client cert validation for the pilot.

Deliverables within 48 hours:
- Updated dataflow diagram that marks in-memory-only components and encryption boundaries (Ben).
- Short logging & retention commitment with one-paragraph operational controls for the pilot (Laura).
- Allowlist + control-plane cert fingerprint and instructions for IAM role setup (me).

If that aligns I'll join the 10:00 AM PT slot to walk the diagram.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Software Engineer, Runtime
Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai

Subject: VaultPay private deployment - data lineage, keystore & retention follow-up

Quick recap from today's discovery with VaultPay and clear asks we need to resolve before the pilot kickoff. Short and actionable — please add owners where noted.

Call summary (VaultPay product + security):
- They want Redwood running inside their AWS VPC (private deployment) with strict egress controls and BYOK for encryption keys.
- Primary data class: transaction events + cardholder metadata (PII). They will not allow raw card PAN in logs; tokenized values only.
- Retention constraint: max 30 days for request/response artifacts; audit logs retained longer in their SIEM but must be redacted.

Open technical questions / asks (owners):
1) Keystore & keys (owner: Karthik)
   - Can we support VaultPay-managed KMS keys (customer-managed CMKs in their account) plus an HSM-backed option?
   - Require confirmation on key-exchange pattern (do they want us to rotate keys or will they handle rotation via IAM?)
2) Dataflow & persistence (owner: Laura)
   - They require no persistent disk copies of PII on Redwood side; ephemeral memory only and optional in-memory KV cache with automatic TTL. Can we guarantee diskless operation?
   - Are there logs we currently keep that would contain PII (generation inputs or full responses)? We need list and redaction options.
3) Network / egress controls (owner: Ben)
   - They will block all outbound traffic except to a narrow set of internal endpoints; we need a definitive IP/hostname allowlist and whether mutual TLS is required to talk to their KMS endpoints.

Next steps I promised on the call:
- Share a simple dataflow diagram and a short table that maps which artifacts Redwood persists, for how long, and where (attachment: redwood-dataflow-draft.png). (Ben)
- Karthik to confirm BYOK patterns we support and HSM options. (Karthik)
- Laura to draft a minimal logging/retention commitment that we can include as part of the pilot MSA addendum. (Laura)

Subject: Re: VaultPay private deployment - data lineage, keystore & retention follow-up

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. A few answers inline and a couple of clarifying asks.

Keystore & keys:
- We will require Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) via our account-level CMKs in AWS KMS. HSM-backed keys (AWS CloudHSM) are preferred for production; pilot can use CMK (customer-managed).
- Rotation: VaultPay will rotate keys on our schedule; Redwood should support CMK ARN references and not perform key material rotation.

Dataflow & persistence:
- No persistent copies of raw PII are allowed to leave our VPC. Tokenization occurs prior to sending to Redwood where possible. If Redwood needs to persist anything for performance (KV cache), it must be encrypted with our CMK and TTL-limited to 24 hours for the pilot.
- Logging: Application logs forwarded to our SIEM need to be redacted (masking for PANs). We prefer that request/response payloads are not logged to disk by default; if logs exist they must be able to be disabled.

Network / egress:
- Allowed egress: none to public internet except to our KMS endpoints (kms.us-west-2.amazonaws.com) and to our internal S3 endpoints for temporary artifacts. We will provide endpoint ARNs.
- mTLS: Yes, mutual TLS for any control-plane calls is required.

Attachments: vaultpay-security-asks.pdf (redlined answers to our initial questionnaire).

Can you confirm by EOD whether Redwood can (a) operate without persisting PII to disk, (b) support CMK ARNs only (no key material transfer), and (c) provide an allowlist for pilot endpoints + cert fingerprint for mTLS?

title: Embeddings packaging, per-vector vs per-token billing, and bulk discounting
preview:
From: Alex Rivera <alex@bluewave.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Embeddings — billing model, dims, and model compatibility

Hi Ava,

Thanks for taking a look here. Quick intro: I lead ML infra at BlueWave AI — we embed product text for retrieval and personalization (~2–5M vectors/month today, projecting up to 40M in 6 months). We’re evaluating Redwood for hosted embeddings. A few concrete questions that would help us scope cost/packaging: 

- Do you bill embeddings per-vector or per-token? If per-vector, how do you treat different dimensionalities (e.g., 512 / 1536 / 4096)? If per-token, is that input tokens only or both input+creation tokens?
- Which embedding models are supported in the hosted plan? Are there clear tiered price differences between smaller models and high-dim models?
- What do your bulk/commit discounts look like? Example: our initial POC would be ~10M vectors over 3 months, followed by steady-state 20–40M/mo if things go well.
- Any packaging options (prepaid vector credits, blended meters, or included free tier) you recommend for a startup scaling like ours?

If you have a rate card or short doc you can share, that’d be great. Also happy to hop on a 30–45m call.

Thanks — Alex
Alex Rivera
Head of ML Infrastructure, BlueWave AI
alex@bluewave.ai
+1 415-555-0192

[Attachment: brief_product_overview.pdf (stub)]

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Alex Rivera <alex@bluewave.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Embeddings — billing model, dims, and model compatibility

Alex — great to meet you (virtually) and thanks for the context. I attached our current embeddings rate card and a quick notes doc. High level answers below — I kept this focused so you can paste into internal models.

1) Billing unit: per-vector is our primary billing abstraction for embeddings on the hosted API. That means you’re billed one unit per returned vector. We separate text preprocessing (input tokens) billing only when customers use our text-generation or tokenization-heavy pipelines; for pure embeddings use, vector units are the right way to model cost.

2) Dimensions and model families: we support multiple embedding families (lightweight 512-d, standard 1536-d, and high-capacity 4096-d variants). Standard pricing on the rate card is anchored to the 1536-d family. Pricing model: base per-vector price for 1536-d, and then multipliers for other dims: 512-d = 0.7x of base, 4096-d = 1.7x. This keeps billing predictable while reflecting compute delta.

3) Model support: for hosted customers we currently provide the curated list in the attachment (Redwood_Embeddings_Rate_Card_v2.pdf). Short list: RW-Embed-Standard (1536-d), RW-Embed-Small (512-d), RW-Embed-High (4096-d). We also support a few community variants for compatibility but those are quoted case-by-case for SLA/throughput reasons.

4) Bulk/commit discounts & packaging: we offer three common options — (a) prepaid vector credits (one-time top-ups usable over 12 months), (b) monthly committed volume discounts (tiered by monthly vectors), and (c) enterprise blended-rate contracts for very large steady-state usage. Example anchor: standard list price is $0.00030 per 1536-d vector; committed tiers can go as low as $0.00018 per vector for 10M+ vectors/month with a 12-month minimum. Prepaid credit pricing usually gives similar / slightly better effective rates depending on term length.

5) Quick POC suggestion: for a 3-month 10M vector POC we typically recommend prepaid credits plus a small initial reserved capacity bump so you see consistent latency. That combination prevents surprises as you ramp.

I’m looping in Karthik from Pricing/Partnerships to help with sketch pricing and a formal quote. I also attached a short comparison sheet that maps dims -> multiplier and an FAQ on model eligibility.

Next steps: would you like a short pricing sketch (sample invoice) and a proposed PO? If yes I can have a draft to you in 24–48 hours. If you prefer a call, I can book Karthik and Marissa (finance) for midweek.

Cheers,
Ava Chen
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com
[Attachment: Redwood_Embeddings_Rate_Card_v2.pdf (stub); link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD-example]

Subject: Embeddings — billing model, dims, and model compatibility

Hi Ava,

Thanks for taking a look here. Quick intro: I lead ML infra at BlueWave AI — we embed product text for retrieval and personalization (~2–5M vectors/month today, projecting up to 40M in 6 months). We’re evaluating Redwood for hosted embeddings. A few concrete questions that would help us scope cost/packaging: 

- Do you bill embeddings per-vector or per-token? If per-vector, how do you treat different dimensionalities (e.g., 512 / 1536 / 4096)? If per-token, is that input tokens only or both input+creation tokens?
- Which embedding models are supported in the hosted plan? Are there clear tiered price differences between smaller models and high-dim models?
- What do your bulk/commit discounts look like? Example: our initial POC would be ~10M vectors over 3 months, followed by steady-state 20–40M/mo if things go well.
- Any packaging options (prepaid vector credits, blended meters, or included free tier) you recommend for a startup scaling like ours?

If you have a rate card or short doc you can share, that’d be great. Also happy to hop on a 30–45m call.

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Alex Rivera <alex@bluewave.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Embeddings — billing model, dims, and model compatibility

Alex — great to meet you (virtually) and thanks for the context. I attached our current embeddings rate card and a quick notes doc. High level answers below — I kept this focused so you can paste into internal models.

1) Billing unit: per-vector is our primary billing abstraction for embeddings on the hosted API. That means you’re billed one unit per returned vector. We separate text preprocessing (input tokens) billing only when customers use our text-generation or tokenization-heavy pipelines; for pure embeddings use, vector units are the right way to model cost.

2) Dimensions and model families: we support multiple embedding families (lightweight 512-d, standard 1536-d, and high-capacity 4096-d variants). Standard pricing on the rate card is anchored to the 1536-d family. Pricing model: base per-vector price for 1536-d, and then multipliers for other dims: 512-d = 0.7x of base, 4096-d = 1.7x. This keeps billing predictable while reflecting compute delta.

3) Model support: for hosted customers we currently provide the curated list in the attachment (Redwood_Embeddings_Rate_Card_v2.pdf). Short list: RW-Embed-Standard (1536-d), RW-Embed-Small (512-d), RW-Embed-High (4096-d). We also support a few community variants for compatibility but those are quoted case-by-case for SLA/throughput reasons.

4) Bulk/commit discounts & packaging: we offer three common options — (a) prepaid vector credits (one-time top-ups usable over 12 months), (b) monthly committed volume discounts (tiered by monthly vectors), and (c) enterprise blended-rate contracts for very large steady-state usage. Example anchor: standard list price is $0.00030 per 1536-d vector; committed tiers can go as low as $0.00018 per vector for 10M+ vectors/month with a 12-month minimum. Prepaid credit pricing usually gives similar / slightly better effective rates depending on term length.

5) Quick POC suggestion: for a 3-month 10M vector POC we typically recommend prepaid credits plus a small initial reserved capacity bump so you see consistent latency. That combination prevents surprises as you ramp.

I’m looping in Karthik from Pricing/Partnerships to help with sketch pricing and a formal quote. I also attached a short comparison sheet that maps dims -> multiplier and an FAQ on model eligibility.

Next steps: would you like a short pricing sketch (sample invoice) and a proposed PO? If yes I can have a draft to you in 24–48 hours. If you prefer a call, I can book Karthik and Marissa (finance) for midweek.

Cheers,
Ava Chen
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com
[Attachment: Redwood_Embeddings_Rate_Card_v2.pdf (stub); link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD-example]

title: Enterprise MAP: Capacity & GPU pool runbook for CloudEdge pilot
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Nguyen <alicia@cloudedge.com>
Cc: Marcus Lee <marcus.lee@cloudedge.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Enterprise MAP: Capacity & GPU pool runbook for CloudEdge pilot

Alicia / Marcus —

Thanks for a great kickoff yesterday. Per our call, attached is a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (MAP) and a lightweight GPU-sizing workbook to help the CloudEdge infra team validate lead times and pool sizing for the Dedicated + Private paths. The goal: enable a 90-day pilot followed by a predictable reserved-capacity conversion if the eval meets p95 latency and qps targets.

High level ask from you this week:
- Confirm a pilot start window (ideally week of May 17) and the preferred deployment mode (Dedicated in us-west vs Private VPC in your AWS account).
- Share expected peak/avg QPS for the evaluation traffic (sample traces are fine).
- Any procurement constraints (PO lead time, net terms) we should account for in the MAP.

Key assumptions in the attached workbook:
- 12M tokens / day peak during eval; 8 concurrent request workers; 1.5s target p95 for 2k-token tail requests.
- Preferred GPU profile: A800 + 48GB workers for model variant A, fallback to 8xA10s for cost tier.
- Delivery lead time (Dedicated pool): 9–11 weeks from signed commitment to pools in prod (hardware + infra + smoke tests). Private (VPC) lead time: 6–8 weeks if we coordinate VPC peering and KMS access ahead of time.

I'll loop in Priyom to help with the sizing and Karthik for the technical runbook. If this looks good we can lock milestones in the MAP and route a DocuSign.

Attachments: RDW_MAP_CloudEdge_v1.2.pdf (MAP draft), GPU-sizing-workbook.xlsx (sizing assumptions), SOC2-evidence-request.pdf (initial security asks)

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0118



From: Alicia Nguyen <alicia@cloudedge.com>

Cc: Marcus Lee <marcus.lee@cloudedge.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Apr 2027 11:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Enterprise MAP: Capacity & GPU pool runbook for CloudEdge pilot

Ben —

Thanks for the doc and the quick turnaround. We reviewed with infra and procurement. A few clarifications before we lock dates:

1) Pilot mode: prefer Private (VPC) with Redwood Private control plane — security requires customer-managed KMS and traffic egress restrictions.
2) Eval traffic: we can commit to a staging replay of ~6M tokens/day avg, peak to 10M tokens/day. We have a 3-week spike window between weeks 2–4 of the pilot (customer demo).
3) Procurement: standard 30-day net, but hardware-onboarding PO might take 5–7 business days once we sign.

Questions:
- Can you confirm the private/VPC lead time estimate and any pre-reqs we should start (VPC CIDR, firewall rules, KMS principal)?
- For the GPU profile, could Priyom propose a conservative and an aggressive pool configuration so our infra lead can triage?

We can target a pilot kickoff of Monday, May 24 if you can accommodate the 6–8 week private lead time.

—Alicia
Alicia Nguyen
Sr. Product Ops, CloudEdge Inc
alicia@cloudedge.com | +1 (206) 555-1299

To: Alicia Nguyen <alicia@cloudedge.com>, Marcus Lee <marcus.lee@cloudedge.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Enterprise MAP: Capacity & GPU pool runbook for CloudEdge pilot

Alicia / Marcus —

Appreciate the quick review. Short answers inline and attached an updated sizing tab with two configurations (Conservative / Aggressive).

- Private lead time: we validated with the infra ops team — 5–7 weeks is realistic if we start the VPC prep now. Key pre-reqs to start immediately:
  * VPC CIDR blocks and VPC peering contact
  * IAM principal for Redwood service (we'll share least-privilege template)
  * KMS key ARN and contact for KMS root admin access to add Redwood service principal
  * Open outbound 443 to our control plane endpoints for health/tracing and metrics

- Pool configs (attached workbook):
  * Conservative: 6xA800 workers (48GB) + 2xA10s (fallback) — estimated steady-state cost during eval: $X/day; supports up to 60 qps for <1k token requests.
  * Aggressive: 12xA800 workers + 4xA10s fallback — supports 140 qps and has headroom for your 3-week spike with autoscaling buffer.

We also added milestone timeline rows to the MAP: VPC prep (week 1–2), infra provisioning and smoke tests (week 3–5), pilot ramp and tuning (week 6–9). I've marked the procurement gating item: PO signed -> config provisioning (target 5 business days).

Next steps I propose:
1) Priyom and Karthik run a 60-minute sizing sync with your infra (we can do Wed 28/Apr 10am PT).
2) CloudEdge confirms VPC/CIDR + KMS contact by EOD Tue 27/Apr.
3) We finalize MAP and send DocuSign for signature by Thu 29/Apr.

CCing Priyom/Karthik to confirm availability.

Thanks,
Ben

title: Renewal + expansion discussion — Eastlake pricing alignment and temporary rate shelter
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
To: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@eastlake.io>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal + expansion discussion — Eastlake pricing alignment

Hi Eleanor —

Thanks again for the call last week. As we head toward Eastlake's July 1 renewal, I wanted to get a formal note on the table with two objectives: lock a renewal rate for the upcoming quarter while also supporting your anticipated volume increase from the new recommendation engine pilot.

High level proposal (attached SOW + pricing workbook):
- 12-month renewal term, effective 2027-07-01.
- Scaled commit: baseline 300k tokens/month commit, scalable to 650k tokens/month with staged step-up at months 3 and 6.
- Temporary rate shelter: 3-month temporary rate hold on per-token list pricing for any overage during ramp (months 1–3 post-renewal).
- One-time onboarding credit to cover professional services for the recommendation pilot (USD 10k).

Can you confirm the timeline for procurement and whether Eastlake's finance team will need a formal quote or SOW to start approvals? If the rate shelter needs to stretch to 6 months, we can discuss but would want to model impact first.

I've attached the SOW (Eastlake_SOW_v3.pdf) and the pricing workbook (eastlake-pricing-model.xlsx) — the workbook includes a few sensitivity tabs for different ramp assumptions.

If it helps, I can loop our sales ops (Ben) and my manager (Hannah) into the next call to lock the details.

Thanks —
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.ai

Attachments:
- Eastlake_SOW_v3.pdf (SOW draft, 6 pages)
- eastlake-pricing-model.xlsx (pricing sensitivity workbook)

From: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@eastlake.io>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Perez <tperez@eastlake.io>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + expansion discussion — Eastlake pricing alignment

Alex —

Appreciate the clear proposal and the workbook. A few items from our side before we bring this to procurement:

1) Rate shelter duration: Our finance team is pushing for a 6-month temporary rate hold on overage pricing to de-risk the pilot ramp; they see material variability in month-to-month usage.
2) Credits: We would need a mechanism for usage credits if we spike above forecast in months 2–4 (not just a short-term hold).
3) Commit sizing: We like the staggered step-up idea, but can you price a scenario where baseline commit is 250k with a guaranteed 6-month runway to scale to 700k?
4) Contracting timeline: Procurement needs finalized SOW + signed paperwork by 2027-06-15 to hit Q3 budget cutoffs.

Tom (cc'd) will be the procurement owner. Can you share a version of the workbook that clearly shows the 6-month hold case and the proposed credit mechanics? Also, any precedent for multi-month rate shelters would help us sell this internally.

Thanks,
Eleanor
Head of Data Platforms | Eastlake Retail
eleanor@eastlake.io

(Short reply acceptable: Tom will coordinate budget approvals.)

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Cc: (internal)
Date: Fri, 23 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Renewal + expansion discussion — Eastlake pricing alignment

Folks — forwarding Eastlake's ask below. Key asks: 6-month temporary rate hold on overages, credit mechanism for spikes, baseline commit tweak (250k -> step to 700k in 6 months), procurement sign-off by 2027-06-15.

My read: Eastlake is committed to growth but needs downside protection while the recommender pilot proves out. They pushed the timeline — procurement wants fully-specified SOW + numbers by mid-June.

Proposed internal options (quick bullets):
- Option A (Conservative): 12-month term, baseline 300k commit, staged step-up to 650k, 3-month temporary rate shelter on overage pricing, USD 10k onboarding credit. Low margin impact.
- Option B (Customer-first): 12-month term, baseline 250k commit, guaranteed escalation to 700k over 6 months, 6-month rate hold on overage pricing, structured spike credits (tiered credit: 50% credit for spike above 120% of forecast in month 1–3; 25% in months 4–6), USD 10k onboarding credit + 1-day white-glove onboarding. Higher revenue risk but likely to close and expand.
- Option C (Bridge): 12-month term with a 3-month rate hold plus a one-time overage credit pool (USD 25k) to be applied to spikes; commit mirrors Option B. Medium impact; easier to quantify.

Operational asks: if we go Option B, we need signoff from finance on the credit pool impact and a DocuSign-ready SOW with explicit credit application rules. Hannah — can you advise on how aggressive we can be on a 6-month hold given current margin targets? Ben — can you run the modeled impact in the pricing workbook (I'll add a '6mo_hold' tab) and share a redlined SOW by Monday?

Quoted reply from Eastlake below for context.

> From: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@eastlake.io>
> To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
> Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -04:00
> Subject: Re: Renewal + expansion discussion — Eastlake pricing alignment
>
> Alex —
>
> Appreciate the clear proposal and the workbook. A few items from our side before we bring this to procurement:
>
> 1) Rate shelter duration: Our finance team is pushing for a 6-month temporary rate hold on overage pricing to de-risk the pilot ramp; they see material variability in month-to-month usage.
> 2) Credits: We would need a mechanism for usage credits if we spike above forecast in months 2–4 (not just a short-term hold).
> 3) Commit sizing: We like the staggered step-up idea, but can you price a scenario where baseline commit is 250k with a guaranteed 6-month runway to scale to 700k?
> 4) Contracting timeline: Procurement needs finalized SOW + signed paperwork by 2027-06-15 to hit Q3 budget cutoffs.

> Tom (cc'd) will be the procurement owner. Can you share a version of the workbook that clearly shows the 6-month hold case and the proposed credit mechanics? Also, any precedent for multi-month rate shelters would help us sell this internally.

Thanks,
> Eleanor
> Head of Data Platforms | Eastlake Retail

Attachments referenced: eastlake-pricing-model.xlsx (needs a 6mo tab)

Alex

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
To: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@eastlake.io>
Cc: Tom Perez <tperez@eastlake.io>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 16:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + expansion discussion — Eastlake pricing alignment

Eleanor / Tom —

Thanks for the clear asks and the procurement timeline. After internal review, here's a revised offer we can take to legal and SOW that balances Eastlake's risk appetite and Redwood's commercial constraints:

Summary of revised offer:
- Term & Commits: 12-month term from 2027-07-01. Baseline commit 250k tokens/month, stepped increases to hit 700k tokens/month by month 6 (milestone-based).
- Temporary Rate Shelter: 4-month rate shelter on per-token overage pricing (effective months 0–3 post-renewal) — we can commit to 4 months rather than 6 to reduce revenue exposure.
- Spike Credits: One-time usage credit pool USD 20k to apply to overage spikes in months 2–6. Credits tiering: 60% credit for overage in month 1 after ramp, 30% in month 2–3, 15% months 4–6. Credits applied against invoice in the following billing cycle and subject to audit.
- Onboarding: USD 10k professional services credit + 1-day onsite/remote kickoff workshop.
- SLAs & Ops: Dedicated onboarding runbook and weekly cadence for first 90 days; optional monitoring hook integration at no charge for the first 30 days.

Next steps:
1) Ben will attach a redlined SOW and updated pricing workbook with a "4mo_shelter_plus_credits" tab by EOD Wed.
2) If this aligns, please confirm by 2027-05-05 so we can route to legal and get a DocuSign envelope out to hit your 2027-06-15 procurement cutoff.

If the 4-month shelter is a hard stop for your finance team, we can escalate to leadership for an exception, but we want to try to keep the standard approval flow.

DocuSign preview (to be generated after your confirmation): https://www.docusign.net/preview/abc123-eastlake

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.ai

Attachments (to follow): eastlake-pricing-model_4mo_shelter.xlsx, SOW_redline_v2.pdf

title: Split billing and feature mapping for multi-team rollout
preview:
From: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Finnotech Procurement <procurement@finnotech.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo last week — Finnotech is excited about Redwood's latency and the KV cache savings for our ingestion pipelines. Before we engage procurement, a couple of packaging/ops questions from our side that will determine which route we take:

1) We have two engineering teams that will consume the service: Payments (latency sensitive, will need Dedicated capacity) and Data Science (research, okay on Hosted). Can Redwood issue split invoices (two invoice entities) under one master account so we can allocate spend to different BU budgets?

2) The Payments team wants the ability to \"swap\" seats between tiers mid-term (e.g., move 10 seats from Hosted Pro to Dedicated Lite), and we need to understand credit/proration rules and whether seat counts can be pooled across projects. How does Redwood handle upgrades/downgrades and partial-term credits?

3) We saw references to a \"feature lattice\" — a compact mapping of which features (streaming, function calling, shard-level isolation, longer context) are in which tier. Could you share the latest mapping and whether feature bundles can be purchased à la carte?

4) Any lift on discounts for committing to a 12-month blended commitment across both teams?

Attached is our internal MAT (high level). Procurement may follow with the standard questionnaire. Curious about timeline — procurement prefers a single agreement with addendum for the Dedicated pool if possible.

Appreciate any concrete options/examples we can show leadership during procurement.

Best,
Ella Novak
Director of AI Platforms
Finnotech

Attachment: Finnotech_MAT.pdf (PDF, 1 page - high-level environment diagram)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Hi Ella — thanks for the clear list. Short answers up front, then a couple of concrete options:

- Split invoicing: supported. We can create invoice aliases under one master account; each alias is a separate invoice (and GL code) and ties back to the same account ID. That covers your BU allocation ask.

- Seat swaps & proration: upgrades (Hosted -> Dedicated or tier upgrade) are prorated; we apply pro-rated credits for the unused portion of the previous tier and bill the delta for the remainder of the term. Downgrades are supported at term boundary only (no mid-term automatic downshift) unless you allow us to apply a small administrative fee. Seat pooling across projects is possible via \"Seat Reserve\" add-on (one-time config). That allows reassigning seats across invoice aliases without multiple invoices — good for dynamic teams.

- Feature lattice: we maintain a matrix; some features (long-context KV cache, regional pinning, dedicated NVMe caching) are Dedicated-only. Streaming & function calling are available on Hosted Pro and Dedicated. We can produce a one-page feature-lattice PDF and an a-la-carte price list; linked is the draft: https://drive.redwood.ai/feature-lattice-v2

- Discounts: yes, we typically provide a blended discount for a 12-month multi-product commit. Exact % depends on total committed spend and whether you take a minimum Dedicated baseline.

Two Suggested Paths (examples you can present to procurement):

1) Master account + invoice aliases (recommended):
   - Create 2 invoice aliases: PAYMENTS (Dedicated pool) and DS (Hosted).
   - Commit to 12 months, 40% of total spend in Dedicated baseline -> eligible for blended 15% discount.
   - Seat Reserve add-on ($2,000 one-time) enables mid-term reassignments between aliases; upgrades prorate automatically.

2) Single invoice, per-project tags:
   - Single invoice with internal cost tags; easier contracting but billing allocation is manual for you.
   - Lower admin overhead but no per-invoice GL separation.

I'll attach a sample quote for option 1 with rough numbers for 10 Dedicated seats + 30 Hosted seats. Want me to loop in finance for an exact commitment quote?

Cheers,
Aditya Rao

Recommended rollout (minimize disruption):

   - Week 1: Mirror API keys and tags, run smoke tests.
   - Month 1–2: Use monthly usage reports to validate tag-to-invoice attribution; claim up to 10 no-cost seat reassignments to cover initial shuffle.

title: Rapid notice: data transfer state alignment and evidence routing
preview:
From: Samir Patel <samir.p@finpulse.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: FinPulse Legal <legal@finpulse.com>, Ava Lopez <ava.lopez@forensica.io>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: RE: notice window — what we need from Redwood

Ben / Kimberly —

Thanks for the quick sync this morning. After our internal triage there are two items Legal and our security team want clarified before we can trigger customer notifications:

1) Notification scope: is the current draft limited to telemetry/telemetry-derived anomalies, or does it include potential exposure of customer content stored in KV cache?
2) Timeline assumptions: your DPA refers to \"reasonable efforts\" and a 72-hour notice window for certain incidents — can you confirm the specific clock start (detection vs. confirmed exfiltration)?
3) Subprocessor involvement: do any of the subprocessors we rely on (as listed in the April inventory) have independent notification obligations that would alter the timeline or add coordination steps?

We also need a single routing point for evidence exports (SFTP / signed transfer) and a simple matrix mapping evidence types to who at Redwood will accept them (forensics, legal, ops). Ava from Forensica is on the thread to confirm format expectations.

If you can provide those clarifications and an updated evidence-routing matrix, we can confirm our public-facing comms timing.

Appreciate urgency — legal is holding on the customer advisory until we have the alignment.

—Samir

Attachments: none


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.p@finpulse.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Ava Lopez <ava.lopez@forensica.io>, FinPulse Legal <legal@finpulse.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: RE: notice window — what we need from Redwood

Samir — thanks, understood. Quick answers up front, then we’ll follow with the matrix and a short call if you want: 

1) Scope: the active incident we referenced is limited to anomalous service telemetry that flagged an unusual outbound transfer from a worker pool. At present we do not see evidence of KV cache extraction of customer content. We are treating content exposure as a potential escalation path and will update if evidence changes.

2) Timeline: for contractual purposes the 72-hour clock in our DPA starts on \"confirmed compromise\" — i.e., when our incident response team has validated unauthorized access to customer data. We will communicate interim discovery notices earlier where we have actionable mitigations or containment completed.

3) Subprocessors: current publicly shared subprocessor list (attached) shows two third parties used for backups and telemetry aggregation. Both have contractual obligations to notify us within 24 hours of any suspected compromise; however, their notifications will not pause our clock. We will incorporate their notices into a consolidated timeline for customers.

Next steps: Kimberley/Kimberly will attach an evidence-routing matrix and a proposed SFTP / signed-hash handoff pattern. Marissa will confirm the legal wording we should use in external notices (specifically how to reference \"reasonable efforts\" and the precise start condition). Grace will own the operations handoff if we need a cross-organization export.

I’ll set a 30-minute alignment call for 11:30 PT unless you prefer otherwise.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0148

Attachments: subprocessor-list-2027-04.pdf


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Samir Patel <samir.p@finpulse.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Ava Lopez <ava.lopez@forensica.io>, FinPulse Legal <legal@finpulse.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Re: RE: notice window — what we need from Redwood

Samir / team —

Attached is the evidence-routing matrix (CSV / XLSX) and an export template we typically use for forensic handoffs. Highlights: 

- Evidence types and initial owner:
  * Service telemetry logs (per instance): SecOps (kimberly.park@redwood.com)
  * Inference request metadata (non-content headers): Product Security (grace.oconnor@redwood.com)
  * Full payloads / KV snapshots: Legal escalation only — require signed PII transfer form + DPA addendum

- Handoff mechanics:
  * Preferred: SFTP + SHA256 manifest (incoming from Redwood to FinPulse for joint cases).
  * Alternate: AWS S3 presigned URL with short TTL + manifest.
  * For high sensitivity (payloads): encrypted disk courier (rare) or direct on-prem export under supervisor sign-off.

- Timing: operationally we can prepare a scoped export within 24 hours of a confirmed ask; full payload extractions (if approved) may take 48–72 hours depending on data size and legal signoffs.

Ava — the CSV includes the column set you requested; please confirm if we need additional hashes or transcript redaction markers.

I’ve attached: evidence-routing-matrix.xlsx and export-template.txt

—Kimberly

Attachments: evidence-routing-matrix.xlsx; export-template.txt


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.p@finpulse.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, FinPulse Legal <legal@finpulse.com>, Ava Lopez <ava.lopez@forensica.io>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Re: Re: RE: notice window — what we need from Redwood

Samir —

Legal quick note to align expectations for customer-facing language and contractual triggers: 

- \"Confirmed compromise\": for DPA wording we will call it \"confirmed unauthorized access to customer data as determined by Redwood's incident response team following forensic validation.\" That keeps the start tied to our validation while remaining clear to customers.
- \"Reasonable efforts\": we will avoid ambiguous public phrasing. Proposed line: \"Redwood will use commercially reasonable and documented incident response procedures to assess, contain, and remediate the incident and will notify Customers as described in the DPA once unauthorized access to Customer Data is confirmed.\"
- Subprocessor cascade: we will state that Redwood coordinates with subprocessors and will include relevant subprocessors in the evidence handoff when their systems are materially implicated. We will not list subprocessors in the public notice unless required by law or the subprocessor's incident materially impacts customer data.

Operational commitments: we can commit to providing an initial customer notice template within 24 hours of confirmation and an evidence summary to FinPulse within 72 hours of confirmation, subject to receipt of any required subprocessor statements.

Ben — happy for you to share the attached matrix as discussed. Samir / Legal — let us know if the confirmation wording needs any revision; we can push a brief redline within the hour.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0192

Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, FinPulse Legal <legal@finpulse.com>, Ava Lopez <ava.lopez@forensica.io>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Re: Re: RE: notice window — what we need from Redwood

Samir —

Legal quick note to align expectations for customer-facing language and contractual triggers: 

- \"Confirmed compromise\": for DPA wording we will call it \"confirmed unauthorized access to customer data as determined by Redwood's incident response team following forensic validation.\" That keeps the start tied to our validation while remaining clear to customers.
- \"Reasonable efforts\": we will avoid ambiguous public phrasing. Proposed line: \"Redwood will use commercially reasonable and documented incident response procedures to assess, contain, and remediate the incident and will notify Customers as described in the DPA once unauthorized access to Customer Data is confirmed.\"
- Subprocessor cascade: we will state that Redwood coordinates with subprocessors and will include relevant subprocessors in the evidence handoff when their systems are materially implicated. We will not list subprocessors in the public notice unless required by law or the subprocessor's incident materially impacts customer data.

Operational commitments: we can commit to providing an initial customer notice template within 24 hours of confirmation and an evidence summary to FinPulse within 72 hours of confirmation, subject to receipt of any required subprocessor statements.

Ben — happy for you to share the attached matrix as discussed. Samir / Legal — let us know if the confirmation wording needs any revision; we can push a brief redline within the hour.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0192

Attachments: updated-notice-draft.txt

title: Mosaic Health - Latency spike & 500s — urgent
preview:
From: Samira Khan <samira.k@mosaichealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Mosaic Health Oncall <oncall@mosaichealth.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: API Latency / 500s starting 08:50 PDT

Hi Ben,

Spoke with our engineering team — starting ~08:50 PDT today we saw request latencies across our production routes climb from ~120ms to 3–5s and a spike of 500 errors. Traffic looks steady from our side and this started suddenly. We’ve routed a small percentage to a fallback that doesn’t call Redwood and errors drop.

Impact: several live patient-facing workflows are timing out. Our support queue has increased by 40%. This is affecting our morning shift. Can you confirm if there’s an ongoing region issue and ETA on mitigation?

I attached a small sample of logs.

Thanks,
Samira
Samira Khan
Lead Platform Engineer, Mosaic Health
samira.k@mosaichealth.com

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Samira Khan <samira.k@mosaichealth.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 09:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Re: API Latency / 500s starting 08:50 PDT

Ben / Marissa / Samira — thanks for the heads up. I’m SRE on-call. Quick summary of what I’m seeing from our telemetry:
- Region: us-west-2 (primary) is showing CPU and request queue growth on the 4x serving pools backing the standard-generic model route.
- Latency: p50/p95 jumped ~25x beginning 08:50 PDT.
- 500 errors map to model-worker OOM/retry cascades when sequence-length distributions spike.

Immediate mitigations in progress (I’ll post updates):
1) Manual traffic steering: start shifting 25% of Mosaic traffic to us-west-1 (warm pool) now — will reduce load on the hot pool.
2) Increase worker count on us-west-2 pool by +30% (provisioning, ETA ~8 mins).
3) Rotate KV cache for that customer to avoid harmful cache blowups (clear prefixes).
4) Enable short-lived quantized fallback model (lower mem) for heavy sequences to reduce tail latencies.

I’ve requested runbook logs and collected recent traces — attaching consolidated logs (logs_20270427.zip) and our runbook excerpt. Estimated stabilization within 25–40 minutes if traffic shift works; if not, we’ll escalate to cross-region full failover.

Aditya Rao
SRE, Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai
Attachment: logs_20270427.zip (application/zip, 8 MB)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>

Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Re: API Latency / 500s starting 08:50 PDT

Ben / Aditya — thanks. I’ve queued a short customer message that we can send once we confirm the first mitigation step succeeds:

"Samira — we’re seeing an elevated error rate tied to sequence spikes for your routes in us-west-2. SRE is shifting a portion of your traffic to a secondary pool and enabling a low-memory fallback model to mitigate timeouts. We will update in 30 minutes with status and next steps. We will also provide a post-incident summary and recommended config changes to avoid recurrence."

Also want to flag Mosaic’s contract has a response SLA and there will likely be credit requests if the incident extends beyond 2 hours. Looping Laura for exec alignment on any needed concessions.

Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

Please hold any external language that promises credits until we confirm scope and duration. I’m also escalating to our CTO briefly to keep execs aware in case Mosaic asks for an executive call.

Laura Bennett
Sales Director, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai | +1 650-555-0144


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samira Khan <samira.k@mosaichealth.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 15:24:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Re: API Latency / 500s starting 08:50 PDT

Update (final):
- Traffic shift to us-west-1 at 09:36 PDT reduced error rate from 6.2% -> 0.4% and p95 latency returned to ~210ms by 09:58 PDT.
- Worker capacity increase and fallback model prevented further OOM cascades.
- Cleared offending KV prefixes; no residual cache hot keys.
- Post-incident checks completed and we’ve left Mosaic pinned to the multi-pool policy to avoid burst saturation.

Samira — I’ll prepare a short postmortem and recommended model/config changes for you by EOD. Thanks for the quick sample logs — they helped us identify the sequence-length pattern fast.

Aditya Rao
SRE, Redwood Inference

title: Atlas Bank - Operational Acceptance & Traffic Phasing Matrix
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sofia Ramos <sofia.ramos@atlasbank.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood onboarding proposal + high-level phasing for Atlas Bank

Hi Sofia —

Thanks again for the RFP review call last week. Attached is our initial onboarding proposal that maps the RFP requirements to a phased implementation and acceptance matrix. Key points up front:

- We propose a three-phase rollout (Sandbox -> Pilot -> Production) with a two-week canary window during Pilot for each production shard.
- Minimum required Atlas engineering involvement: weekly syncs during Pilot, ops on-call during the canary window, and pre-cutover smoke tests.
- Fallback: traffic reversion to the previous model variant + read-only mode for dependent services.

I've attached: Redwood_Atlas_Onboarding_Proposal_v1.pdf (proposal + summary) and MAM-implementation-checklist.xlsx (detailed gates). A single-sheet timeline (phase_plan_v1.xlsx) is also attached in the follow-up email.

Request: Can you confirm the stakeholders who should be in the Pilot acceptance calls, and whether your procurement team requires a specific SLA appendix in this proposal?

Available next steps: technical sync (Priyom to lead), procurement redline review, then target dates for a Pilot start window.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Redwood_Atlas_Onboarding_Proposal_v1.pdf (application/pdf), MAM-implementation-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet/xlsx)

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@atlasbank.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 12:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood onboarding proposal + high-level phasing for Atlas Bank

Ben —

Thanks for the fast turnaround and clear docs. A couple of clarifying questions before we push to procurement: 

1) Can you give a concrete example of the Pilot canary acceptance criteria (metrics + thresholds)? We need CPU/latency/accuracy thresholds to include in our checklist.
2) For the fallback mode, what is the expected RTO (minutes) to get services back to read-only, and do you support automated rollback triggered by latency or error-rate thresholds?

Procurement is copied here; they asked for a concise timeline and any third-party integrators that will be involved (CloudBridge was mentioned on our call).

Sofia Ramos
Director, Digital Channels — Atlas Bank
sofia.ramos@atlasbank.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sofia Ramos <sofia.ramos@atlasbank.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, CloudBridge Consulting <eng@cloudbridge.io>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood onboarding proposal + high-level phasing for Atlas Bank

Sofia — thanks, great questions. Summary answers and an attached detailed phase plan (phase_plan_v1.xlsx):

Pilot canary acceptance (example):
- Latency: 95th percentile p95 < 180ms for end-to-end request (API gateway -> Redwood).
- Error rate: 5xx < 0.1% sustained over 10 minutes.
- Quality: baseline prompt set pass rate >= 98% (per your eval rubric); we can run a shadow eval set during Pilot.
- Resource: GPU saturation < 75% sustained across the pool during canary run.

Rollback & RTO:
- Automated rollback trigger: yes — we support policy-based automatic rollback (latency/error thresholds).
- RTO: Typical automated reversion to read-only or previous variant is < 10 minutes if rollback policy is triggered and fallthrough routing completes. Manual remediation timeline depends on the root cause.

Third-party integrator: CloudBridge will handle network peering and VPC routing for the private deployment. Priyom will coordinate the handoff and list the exact networking artifacts required (peer ranges, gateway details).

I've attached the phase_plan_v1.xlsx which maps dates to gating criteria. Proposed calendar: Pilot start 2027-05-10, two-week canary per shard, full production cutover targeted 2027-06-07 (contingent on successful canaries).

If that looks reasonable I'll update the proposal with the SLA appendix and a more prescriptive canary runbook.

Ben
