title: Incident response: proposed notification windows and cooperation language
preview:
From: Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Evan Park <evan.park@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Ben —

Thanks again for taking time last week. As we move toward finalizing the DPA redlines, our legal and privacy teams asked us to confirm Redwood's incident-notification windows and what level of operational cooperation we can expect during an investigation. Two specific asks from Nimbus:

1) Notification window: we need written notice of any confirmed data breach affecting Nimbus personal data within 24 hours of Redwood confirming the incident.
2) Active cooperation: a point-of-contact and access to investigation artifacts (logs, export of affected records) within 72 hours after notice so our forensics team can validate impact and mitigation.

Attached is a proposed redline that highlights the clauses (DPA_proposed_redline.pdf) and a simple matrix of evidence types and timing (incident_cooperation_matrix.xlsx).

We recognize law enforcement may request temporary restraint on public disclosure; however, we need a firm internal timeline and a commitment to coordinate customer-facing messaging within the stated windows.

Can you confirm Redwood can accept the 24/72 cadence or propose acceptable alternative language?

Thanks,
Dana Ortiz
Chief Privacy Officer
Nimbus Health



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Dana —

Appreciate the clear asks and the redline. Looping in Hannah (Security Ops) and Laura (Legal/privacy) to weigh in on feasibility. High level from my side: we want to be responsive, but we also need to ensure we don't commit to timelines that prevent proper validation (false positives) or conflict with law enforcement requests.

Hannah/Laura — can you review the attached redline and the 24/72 expectation and propose language that balances Nimbus's needs with our operational constraints? Key constraints to consider:
- We typically need an initial triage to confirm a material incident (30–48 hours in many cases).
- Some forensics artifacts require internal approvals and may be subject to data minimization or redaction.
- We have existing SOC 2 playbook commitments which we should align with.

Proposed next step: Hannah drafts a short playbook excerpt with concrete timelines and escalation contacts; Laura prepares a legal fallback clause (e.g., \"as soon as reasonably practicable\" with a maximum timeframe).

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com



From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 11:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Dana / team —

Thanks — I reviewed the matrix and redline. Operationally, we can commit to the following, which I think is a pragmatic compromise between your ask and our SRE/forensics constraints:

- Initial notification (\"acknowledgement\"): within 24 hours of us confirming that an event meets our incident triage threshold (we will call this \"confirmed security incident\"). This is an informational notice that we have opened an incident and are investigating.

- Substantive impact notice: within 72 hours of confirmation, we will provide a substantive update that includes: scope summary, affected data categories, estimated user count (if available), and provisional mitigation steps. If the investigation is ongoing and details are preliminary, we'll clearly mark items as \"under investigation\".

- Evidence sharing: we can provide exportable investigation artifacts (logs, hashed identifiers, timeline of events) within 7 calendar days after substantive notice. Some raw artifacts (live packet captures, full system images) require internal review or legal redaction; we'll provide redacted versions earlier where possible.

- Law enforcement hold: where a non-disclosure is requested by law enforcement, we will notify Nimbus of the hold (to the extent permitted) and provide an estimated window when the hold will be lifted. We cannot commit to breaching a lawful order.

I attached a one-page playbook excerpt to this message (inline below) and I can convert to a paragraph for the DPA. If Nimbus requires evidence within 72 hours without any exceptions, that's likely to be a hard no; our minimal workable timeline for artifact provision is 7 days.

H — security ops manager
Hannah Schmitt
Security Operations
Redwood Inference



From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Dana —

Thanks for the clear framing. We've reviewed Nimbus's redline and Hannah's operational proposal. Below is suggested DPA clause language that aims to be specific while preserving necessary operational and legal safeguards. Please treat as negotiable text — happy to refine.

Suggested clause (draft):

\"In the event that Redwood becomes aware of a confirmed security incident affecting personal data of Nimbus customers, Redwood shall: (a) provide initial written notice to Nimbus within twenty-four (24) hours of confirming the incident; (b) provide a substantive written update within seventy-two (72) hours of confirmation, including impacted data categories, estimated scope, and preliminary mitigation measures; and (c) use commercially reasonable efforts to provide investigation artifacts and exportable logs necessary for Nimbus to conduct independent validation within seven (7) calendar days of the substantive update.

If Redwood is legally prohibited from providing certain information (including by a law enforcement request), Redwood shall notify Nimbus of the prohibition to the extent permitted by law and shall provide a reasonable estimate of when the prohibition is expected to be lifted. Where information requires redaction to protect third-party privacy or system security, Redwood will provide a redacted version promptly and will make unredacted versions available only pursuant to a lawful process or mutual agreement.

For clarity, \"confirmation\" means Redwood's security team has completed its initial triage and concluded that an incident impacts confidentiality, integrity, or availability of Nimbus personal data. The parties agree to document the incident timeline and communications in the joint incident log referenced in Appendix B.\"

A few legal notes:
- \"Confirmation\" must be defined (Hannah's triage threshold is appropriate).
- If Nimbus requires raw artifacts sooner, we can consider an addendum for expedited legal review (e.g., via Data Access Agreement + NDAs).
- Consider a reciprocal obligation for Nimbus to notify Redwood if they become aware of a related incident on their side that materially affects joint customers.

Next steps: if Nimbus accepts this draft we can fold into the DPA redline. If you want stronger evidence timelines, Laura, we should document the expedited process as a separate exhibit that triggers additional fees / legal safeguards.

Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Security
Redwood Inference



From: Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Laura/Hannah —

Thanks — the proposed clause is much clearer than our initial ask and the definition of \"confirmation\" helps. A couple of follow-ups from the Nimbus privacy team:

- We prefer artifact access within 72 hours where the impact is high (PHI/SSNs) — can we add a severity-based expedited lane where high-severity incidents (as defined in Appendix C) trigger the 72-hour artifact commitment?
- For cases where law enforcement imposes a hold, please include a commitment to provide at least a weekly status update to Nimbus and a target calendar date for lifting the hold when feasible.

If you can accept a severity-based expedited lane and the weekly update for holds, we can sign off and proceed to DPA execution. Otherwise, please flag specific concerns.

Dana Ortiz
Chief Privacy Officer
Nimbus Health
text:
From: Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Evan Park <evan.park@nimbushealth.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Ben —

Thanks again for taking time last week. As we move toward finalizing the DPA redlines, our legal and privacy teams asked us to confirm Redwood's incident-notification windows and what level of operational cooperation we can expect during an investigation. Two specific asks from Nimbus:

1) Notification window: we need written notice of any confirmed data breach affecting Nimbus personal data within 24 hours of Redwood confirming the incident.
2) Active cooperation: a point-of-contact and access to investigation artifacts (logs, export of affected records) within 72 hours after notice so our forensics team can validate impact and mitigation.

Attached is a proposed redline that highlights the clauses (DPA_proposed_redline.pdf) and a simple matrix of evidence types and timing (incident_cooperation_matrix.xlsx).

We recognize law enforcement may request temporary restraint on public disclosure; however, we need a firm internal timeline and a commitment to coordinate customer-facing messaging within the stated windows.

Can you confirm Redwood can accept the 24/72 cadence or propose acceptable alternative language?

Thanks,
Dana Ortiz
Chief Privacy Officer
Nimbus Health



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Dana —

Appreciate the clear asks and the redline. Looping in Hannah (Security Ops) and Laura (Legal/privacy) to weigh in on feasibility. High level from my side: we want to be responsive, but we also need to ensure we don't commit to timelines that prevent proper validation (false positives) or conflict with law enforcement requests.

Hannah/Laura — can you review the attached redline and the 24/72 expectation and propose language that balances Nimbus's needs with our operational constraints? Key constraints to consider:
- We typically need an initial triage to confirm a material incident (30–48 hours in many cases).
- Some forensics artifacts require internal approvals and may be subject to data minimization or redaction.
- We have existing SOC 2 playbook commitments which we should align with.

Proposed next step: Hannah drafts a short playbook excerpt with concrete timelines and escalation contacts; Laura prepares a legal fallback clause (e.g., \"as soon as reasonably practicable\" with a maximum timeframe).

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com



From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 11:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Dana / team —

Thanks — I reviewed the matrix and redline. Operationally, we can commit to the following, which I think is a pragmatic compromise between your ask and our SRE/forensics constraints:

- Initial notification (\"acknowledgement\"): within 24 hours of us confirming that an event meets our incident triage threshold (we will call this \"confirmed security incident\"). This is an informational notice that we have opened an incident and are investigating.

- Substantive impact notice: within 72 hours of confirmation, we will provide a substantive update that includes: scope summary, affected data categories, estimated user count (if available), and provisional mitigation steps. If the investigation is ongoing and details are preliminary, we'll clearly mark items as \"under investigation\".

- Evidence sharing: we can provide exportable investigation artifacts (logs, hashed identifiers, timeline of events) within 7 calendar days after substantive notice. Some raw artifacts (live packet captures, full system images) require internal review or legal redaction; we'll provide redacted versions earlier where possible.

- Law enforcement hold: where a non-disclosure is requested by law enforcement, we will notify Nimbus of the hold (to the extent permitted) and provide an estimated window when the hold will be lifted. We cannot commit to breaching a lawful order.

I attached a one-page playbook excerpt to this message (inline below) and I can convert to a paragraph for the DPA. If Nimbus requires evidence within 72 hours without any exceptions, that's likely to be a hard no; our minimal workable timeline for artifact provision is 7 days.

H — security ops manager
Hannah Schmitt
Security Operations
Redwood Inference



From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Dana Ortiz <dana.ortiz@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

Dana —

Thanks for the clear framing. We've reviewed Nimbus's redline and Hannah's operational proposal. Below is suggested DPA clause language that aims to be specific while preserving necessary operational and legal safeguards. Please treat as negotiable text — happy to refine.

Suggested clause (draft):

\"In the event that Redwood becomes aware of a confirmed security incident affecting personal data of Nimbus customers, Redwood shall: (a) provide initial written notice to Nimbus within twenty-four (24) hours of confirming the incident; (b) provide a substantive written update within seventy-two (72) hours of confirmation, including impacted data categories, estimated scope, and preliminary mitigation measures; and (c) use commercially reasonable efforts to provide investigation artifacts and exportable logs necessary for Nimbus to conduct independent validation within seven (7) calendar days of the substantive update.

If Redwood is legally prohibited from providing certain information (including by a law enforcement request), Redwood shall notify Nimbus of the prohibition to the extent permitted by law and shall provide a reasonable estimate of when the prohibition is expected to be lifted. Where information requires redaction to protect third-party privacy or system security, Redwood will provide a redacted version promptly and will make unredacted versions available only pursuant to a lawful process or mutual agreement.

For clarity, \"confirmation\" means Redwood's security team has completed its initial triage and concluded that an incident impacts confidentiality, integrity, or availability of Nimbus personal data. The parties agree to document the incident timeline and communications in the joint incident log referenced in Appendix B.\"

A few legal notes:
- \"Confirmation\" must be defined (Hannah's triage threshold is appropriate).
- If Nimbus requires raw artifacts sooner, we can consider an addendum for expedited legal review (e.g., via Data Access Agreement + NDAs).
- Consider a reciprocal obligation for Nimbus to notify Redwood if they become aware of a related incident on their side that materially affects joint customers.

Next steps: if Nimbus accepts this draft we can fold into the DPA redline. If you want stronger evidence timelines, Laura, we should document the expedited process as a separate exhibit that triggers additional fees / legal safeguards.

Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Security
Redwood Inference

Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial ask: notification windows + cooperation expectations

- We prefer artifact access within 72 hours where the impact is high (PHI/SSNs) — can we add a severity-based expedited lane where high-severity incidents (as defined in Appendix C) trigger the 72-hour artifact commitment?
- For cases where law enforcement imposes a hold, please include a commitment to provide at least a weekly status update to Nimbus and a target calendar date for lifting the hold when feasible.

If you can accept a severity-based expedited lane and the weekly update for holds, we can sign off and proceed to DPA execution. Otherwise, please flag specific concerns.

preview:
From: Claire Benton <claire@rapidapps.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-19T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Clarify SOC 2 Type II coverage and potential bridge letter

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the SOC 2 materials you shared yesterday. A couple of quick clarifications from our auditors at RapidApps before we proceed:

1) Our requested report period is Jan 1, 2027 — Mar 31, 2027. Can you confirm whether Redwood interprets the coverage as inclusive of events up to 23:59:59 UTC on Mar 31 (i.e., are end-of-day events on Mar 31 treated as in-period)?

2) We had a planned migration on Apr 2 that produced a small set of control evidence for Apr 1–Apr 8. Since April is outside the Type II window, would Redwood be able to provide a bridge letter covering Apr 1–Apr 15 (signed) that attests to continuity of controls during that small window? Our auditors ask for a signed bridge letter rather than a draft if possible.

3) For SOC evidence sampling we may request a point-in-time export for Feb 15, 2027 (UTC). Can you tell me whether you can produce log extracts for that single timestamp and in what format? CSV is preferred.

Timing: our audit timeline is tight — if we can get confirmation on inclusivity and a signed bridge letter by Apr 22 that would keep us on schedule.

Attaching our intake form for reference.

Thanks —
Claire Benton
Compliance Lead, RapidApps Inc.

Attachment: RapidApps_Intake_Form_98721.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Benton <claire@rapidapps.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-20T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Clarify SOC 2 Type II coverage and potential bridge letter

Hi Claire —

Appreciate the clear ask. Short answers up front:
- Yes — Redwood's SOC 2 Type II period Jan 1, 2027 through Mar 31, 2027 is inclusive of activity up to 23:59:59 UTC on Mar 31. The auditor report and our evidence sampling treat that timestamp as in-scope.
- We can provide a signed bridge letter covering Apr 1–Apr 15, 2027 that explains the limited migration activity and the continuity of controls. I will route for legal sign-off and return a signed PDF.
- We can produce a point-in-time log export for Feb 15, 2027 (UTC). Typical export format is CSV with timestamp, event_type, user_id (redacted as requested), request_id, and resource. If you need additional fields let me know.

I've attached the SOC 2 report we have for Q1 and a draft bridge letter for your auditors to review. Also included is an internal spec for log exports so you can confirm the CSV columns we will produce. If RapidApps needs a different timezone for the point-in-time, tell me which timezone and we'll translate timestamps accordingly.

Proposed mutual action plan:
- Apr 20: Redwood sends signed bridge letter + SOC 2 PDF + sample log extract.
- Apr 21: RapidApps confirms acceptance or requests edits.
- Apr 22: Final signed materials delivered.

If that timeline works I'll start the legal loop now. Would you like a quick 20-minute call to walk auditors through the bridge language?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

Ben —

Thanks, that helps. A couple of follow-ups:

If you can send a sample CSV row for Feb 15 so our auditor can validate the schema that would speed things up.

Claire

title: BrightHarbor security questionnaire - encryption control artifacts and operator attestation
preview:
From: Sanjay Patel <sanjay.patel@brightharbor.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@brightharbor.com
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: BrightHarbor security questionnaire - encryption control artifacts and operator attestation

Hi Ben / Kimberly,

Thanks for taking this through — BrightHarbor is working toward SOC 2 Type II and the external auditor has asked for additional artifacts for our SIG/CAIQ review specifically around key management and HSM controls. Attached is the SIG worksheet with highlighted questions (attachment: BrightHarbor_SIG_questions_v3.xlsx).

Top asks from the auditor:
- HSM vendor attestation and any recent third-party attestation reports (e.g., vendor-signed statements).
- Representative KMS rotation logs for the last 6 months (sanitized if necessary).
- Operator/access matrix showing separation of duties for key custodians.
- Evidence of encryption-in-transit (TLS versions/cipher suites) and at-rest envelope key usage.

Can Redwood provide a packaged response and sample artifacts? Ideally we need a delivery by end-of-day Wednesday (2027-04-21) so the auditor can include it in their pre-audit review. If anything needs an NDA or DPA amendment, let us know.

Sanjay
Sanjay Patel
Head of Information Security | BrightHarbor Inc.
mobile: +1-415-555-0192


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay.patel@brightharbor.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: BrightHarbor security questionnaire - encryption control artifacts and operator attestation

Hi Sanjay — thanks for the clear list. We can put together an artifact pack that covers the items you listed. Proposed plan:

1) Kimberly will prepare the formal CAIQ/SIG mapping and include a short narrative for each highlighted question.
2) Jonas will prepare a sanitized rotation-log sample and an ops note describing our rotation procedures and audit trail exports.
3) We'll include our HSM attestation (vendor statement) and a short operator matrix showing role separation.
4) Deliver the package (secure S3 link + DocuSign envelope for any statements) by EOD Wednesday, 2027-04-21.

If you prefer a quick sync to confirm scope, I can set up a 30-minute slot tomorrow.

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Solutions 
Redwood Inference


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay.patel@brightharbor.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: BrightHarbor security questionnaire - encryption control artifacts and operator attestation

Thanks — a few technical notes to make sure artifacts match what you expect:

- HSM: For hosted Redwood customers we use AWS KMS backed by AWS CloudHSM for tenant CMKs; for Dedicated/Private deployments we support customer-provided HSMs via PKCS#11. For the attestation in this pack we will include a vendor-signed attestation we received from our HSM partner (Thales Luna SA 7.1) for the infrastructure HSM cluster used in our Dedicated fleet. That attestation covers firmware version and tamper evidence checks as of 2026-11.

- Key lifecycle / rotation: Redwood performs automated periodic rewrapping of envelope keys (CMK rotation) every 90 days by default for hosted-managed CMKs; customers can request different cadence. Stored rotation logs include: timestamp, key_id (hashed/salted in delivery), rotation_type (scheduled/forced), operator_id (redacted to role level), and a signed audit digest.

- Operator separation: we enforce separation via RBAC + signed shift logs; key custodians do not have deploy privileges. We'll provide an access matrix sample with role descriptions (e.g., KMS Admin, KMS Auditor, Platform Operator).

title: InvestorCare — post-call cost recap & host comparison
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha@investorcare.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-17T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: InvestorCare — post-call recap + pricing asks

Hi Samantha,

Thanks for taking time today — good call. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can prepare a focused proposal: 

High-level usage you shared
- Active users: ~12k monthly end-users (chat + structured prompts)
- Estimated monthly tokens (on current flows): 80M–140M (peak monthly)
- Expected growth: +25% q/q first two quarters after rollout

Budget / targets you mentioned
- Target monthly spend range: $1k–$4k to start (POC) but procurement has a stretch target for production budgeting
- Internal finance target: unit-rate ideally below $1,000 per 1M tokens when scaled (you said procurement would push back unless we can show economics)

Our quick shading on what that implies
- At 100M tokens/month, $1k per 1M => $100k/mo; that’s likely above your initial POC budget but aligns with production unit economics you referenced
- If we look at smaller starter pilots (10–20M/month) we can structure a credit-backed pilot to prove latency and quality before committing to a production price

Host / model cost comparison (public comps — ballpark)
- OpenAI (gpt-family hosting): ~$1.20–$4.00 per 1M tokens depending on model & mix (chat + embeddings omitted here for clarity)
- Anthropic / Claude-style hosted: ~$2.50–$6.50 per 1M tokens for higher-context models
- Open-model hosts / self-hosted (optimized runtime): $0.40–$1.50 per 1M tokens depending on quantization, batching, and caching

What we recommend for next steps
1) Can you confirm procurement’s acceptable monthly spend range for POC vs production (email to procurement cc'd is fine)?
2) Please share any non-negotiable SLA/uptime or data retention requirements — affects dedicated vs hosted pricing.
3) We’ll attach a simple cost-sensitivity sheet showing breakpoints for $/1M at 10–200M tokens and two rollout profiles (pilot -> prod).

Attached: discovery notes + cost sensitivity workbook.

I’ll loop Ben and Karthik in on a tailored pilot proposal once we get procurement’s guardrails.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: 20270417-investorcare-discovery-notes.pdf (PDF), investorcare-cost-sensitivity.xlsx (XLSX)


From: Samantha Lee <samantha@investorcare.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Ruiz <tom.ruiz@investorcare.com>, procurement@investorcare.com
Date: 2027-04-17T16:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: InvestorCare — post-call recap + pricing asks

Ava —

Thanks for the thorough recap. Quick clarifications from our side: 
- Procurement told me POC budget is realistically $2k–$6k for the next 60 days. Production budgeting is separate and will require a strong case (SLOs + cost projections).
- Finance meant ~$800–$1,200 per 1M tokens as a target they’d like to see for long-term pricing, not necessarily a firm commitment.
- We do have a compliance requirement for SOC2 type II evidence and an option to run in a VPC for PHI-like flows (we can share more details).

I’ll have procurement send a short note this week with the formal POC cap. Appreciate the cost sheet, will review and flag questions.

—Samantha
Head of Product, InvestorCare

On Thu, Apr 17, 2027 at 2:05 PM Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (see above)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha@investorcare.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T09:10:00-07:00
Subject: RE: InvestorCare — post-call recap + pricing asks

Looping in a quick internal note before we draft the pilot pricing: 

Ava — thanks for the write-up. A few thoughts on comps / positioning: 
- We should put three tiers in the pilot proposal: Hosted (fastest onboarding), Dedicated reserved (predictable latency, higher commit), Private/VPC (compliance + PHI). Pricing will differ materially.
- For the host comparisons in the outgoing sheet, let's show a 3-column view: OpenAI (public list prices), Anthropic (public), Redwood-optimized self-hosted (our projection). Use conservative runtime assumptions (60% effective batching, 40% cache hit for common prefixes) to avoid overpromising.
- Target for InvestorCare: given finance's ~800–1200 target, propose a pilot effective rate of $1,100 per 1M with credits applied (net to customer $600–$800 during POC) and a path to $500–$700 at scale (100M+/mo) — if they commit to a 6–12 month reserved capacity.

Request to Ava / Karthik:
- Ava: refine cost-sensitivity sheet to include net pricing after credits and show the break-even commit level.
- Karthik: can you draft a short two-slide technical appendix explaining batching/quantization assumptions (so procurement understands why open-host rates diverge)?

I’ll assemble the proposed pilot SOW and forward to Samantha once we have the updated sheet.

—Ben
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference


From: procurement@investorcare.com
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha@investorcare.com>, Tom Ruiz <tom.ruiz@investorcare.com>
Date: 2027-04-21T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: InvestorCare — post-call recap + pricing asks

Hi all,

Confirmed POC cap: $5,000 for a 60-day pilot.
Must-haves for the POC:
- SOC2 Type II evidence or a path to provide it for the pilot (summary report is fine)
- Data isolation via VPC or clear contractual language on data residency
- A simple success metric: <30% drop in user-reported latency vs current baseline AND end-to-end false-positive rate for the main NLP classifier within 2% of current thresholds.

On pricing: procurement is comfortable with a net effective POC rate up to $1,000 per 1M tokens if credits cover the gap to our standard terms; for production we'll need a clear runway to $500–$800 per 1M at 100M+ tokens with reserved commitment.

Next step: please send a one-page pilot SOW + cost sheet with the credits applied by EOD Friday (we'd like that before our internal review next week).

Thanks,
Procurement Team
InvestorCare

title: Telescope post-demo: model evaluation plan & acceptance criteria
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Moreno <ava.moreno@telescope.ai>, Kai Liu <kai.liu@telescope.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: initial benchmark plan and next steps

Hi Ava / Kai,

Thanks again for taking the demo earlier — appreciate the clear walkthrough of the query patterns and the two high-volume endpoints (inference: search-summarize; assist: multi-turn QA). Suggested next steps from our side so we can set up a focused eval run: 

1) Redwood runs a 3-model comparison on your representative prompt bundle (attached prompt-set-v1.jsonl):
   - Open candidate: Llama-3-13B-QLoRA (optimized with Redwood quant config)
   - RedTeam / Redwood curated: Redwood-Atlas-7B-opt (fast path, lower mem)
   - Customer baseline: InternalLarge v1 (your proprietary baseline provided in logs)

2) Evaluation metrics we'll produce: token-level latency (p50/p95), cost per 1k tokens, domain F1 (QA), top-line hallucination rate (measured by hallucination-check suite), and end-to-end throughput under 120ms SLO target.

3) Acceptance thresholds proposal (we can adjust once we see your initial logs):
   - Domain F1 >= 0.78 compared to baseline
   - Hallucination rate <= 3% absolute (measured on your test prompts)
   - p95 latency < 180ms on a single dedicated GPU instance
   - Token cost <= 0.85x baseline (goal: reduce unit cost while matching quality)

If those acceptance thresholds look reasonable, we'll kick off a 10-day sandbox trial (isolated, dedicated capacity) and share a CSV of per-prompt outputs + a short readout. I've attached the prompt bundle and will follow up with a short statement of work (SOW) that covers trial scope.

Could you confirm: 1) a contact for granting sandbox/data access; 2) the baseline model artifacts (if you can share weights or an RPC endpoint) or logs if not; 3) any disallowed behaviors/guardrails to include in the eval (sensitive entities, PII rules, safety blockers)?

Best,
Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: prompt-set-v1.jsonl (19 prompts)


From: Ava Moreno <ava.moreno@telescope.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kai Liu <kai.liu@telescope.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: initial benchmark plan and next steps

Thanks Ben — this is helpful.

We can grant sandbox access once your SOW is signed. Procurement looped in (procurement@telescope.ai) for the SOW. Quick answers:
- Baseline: we can provide logs and a lightweight RPC endpoint for InternalLarge v1 (no weights). I attached a small anonymized sample trace (telescope-benchmark-results.csv) in this reply.
- Guardrails: please strip/gap anonymized PII if you return full-text outputs; we prefer a 60-day retention window during the trial.

Acceptance thresholds look directionally fair, but our leadership asked for an additional regression check on structured field extraction (we use the model to pull address, company-name, and invoice-id from messy input). Can you include a per-field precision/recall metric for the trial?

Appreciate it — Ava
Product Lead, Telescope AI

preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Cho <sarah@healthvect.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-18T14:22:00Z
Subject: Inference fit pulse: bench links & Redwood profiles

Hi Sarah — thanks again for the time earlier. Quick recap and recommended next steps based on our discovery:

Context: HealthVect looking to add a document-QA assistant over PHI-adjacent clinical notes (redaction + retrieval). Goal is sub-500ms P95 for user-facing queries and sustainable throughput for ~30 QPS burst (mostly short queries, avg token output ~60).

Immediate recommendations (pilot scope):
- Candidate models: redwood/rlm-13b-q4 (primary), open/llama-2-13b-8bit (fallback), redwood/opt-30b-int8 (for higher-quality QA if needed).
- Serving profile: medium batch sizing, prefix KV caching for repeated document contexts, sequence cap 768 for QA pipeline.
- SLO targets to validate in pilot: sustained 25 TPS for seq<=512; P95 latency under 350ms at target QPS.

What I’m sending you after this email:
- Redwood-verified perf profiles and an annotated perf snapshot for a comparable healthcare retrieval workload (see attachments).
- A prompt set and evaluation prompts that mirror your examples (prompt pack).
- Links to the model profiles in the Redwood Console so your engineers can inspect CPU/GPU metrics, P95/P99 latency curves, and token-level cost estimates.

Next steps:
1) I can spin up a 2-week pilot with dedicated burst capacity and a canary route to avoid impacting prod traffic — proposed start: next Monday.
2) If procurement needs an intake packet I’ll loop in contracts (Procurement email would be helpful).
3) Technical sync: 30 minutes to align on dataset redaction, metrics, and acceptance criteria. Suggested slots: Tue/Wed morning PST.

Attachments: healthvect_prompt_set_v1.json (prompt examples), perf_snapshot_healthvect_20270419.csv (bench snapshot), redwood_verified_profiles_healthvect.pdf (profile summary).

Let me know preferred pilot start and who from your side should own acceptance.

—
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0143

title: Dedicated capacity follow-up — GPU preference, 95th-pt TPS & token mix (next steps)
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sam Reed <sam@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity follow-up — GPU preference, 95th-pt TPS & token mix (next steps)

Sam — thanks again for the demo and the quick discovery session earlier. Wanted to capture the call recap and a short list of asks so we can size a dedicated pool that meets BrightBridge's SLA targets. Short version + ask list below.

Recap (what we heard)
- Deployment mode: leaning toward Dedicated capacity for predictable throughput and data isolation. Potential later move to Private VPC if compliance gating requires it.
- Primary workloads: synchronous RAG + multi-turn chat and programmatic generation endpoints.
- Business target: real-time inference for user-facing features — tight p95 latency expectations.

Open items we need to size Dedicated properly (please confirm):
1) TPS profile: can you confirm sustained TPS target vs short burst? In the call you mentioned ~200 TPS sustained, but said you can spike up to ~600 TPS for ~3-7 minutes during peak launches. Please confirm 95th/99th traffic percentiles and typical burst duration.
2) Latency SLOs: clarify p95 token latency and target end-to-end p95 for the generation call (you referenced ~300–400ms in the demo).
3) Token mix: average prompt tokens vs average completion tokens, and 95th-percentile sequence lengths (e.g., prompt avg 100, response avg 300 — please provide numbers or sample distribution). Also let us know if you use streaming or require full-completion buffers.
4) Concurrency vs batching: do you prefer lower-latency (fewer micro-batches) or are you ok with batching to improve throughput? Any internal expectation for max concurrent requests per logical endpoint?
5) Model & GPU preference: you mentioned H100 interest for latency headroom — are you flexible with A100 if cost sensitivity is higher? We can present a cost vs p95-latency tradeoff matrix.
6) Region & network: preferred region is us-west-2 per notes; confirm and provide any VPC/peering constraints if you anticipate Private deployment later.

Attachments & next steps
- I attached a short capacity workbook (dedicated_tps_template.xlsx) — if you can fill the highlighted fields (TPS percentiles, token averages, burst windows) we’ll run a quick sizing exercise and return a recommended GPU mix and pool sizing.
- Proposed next engagement: a 45-min technical deep-dive with our infra team (Ben + Priyom) to review perf assumptions and test plan. Proposed times: Thu 22 Apr 10:00am PT or Fri 23 Apr 1:00pm PT. Which works?

If you prefer, we can also run an initial perf test on a small sample (we just need a representative prompt set and consent to run tokenized throughput trials against an isolated test model).

Thanks — I’ll consolidate the workbook results and draft a Mutual Action Plan once we have the numbers.

Best,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192
Redwood Inference

Attachments: dedicated_tps_template.xlsx, mutual_action_plan.md


From: Sam Reed <sam@brightbridge.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@brightbridge.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 16:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity follow-up — GPU preference, 95th-pt TPS & token mix (next steps)

Thanks, Avery — great recap. Confirming the numbers below so you can run the sizing worksheet:

- Deployment mode: yes, Dedicated for initial rollout. We may consider Private VPC for PHI-sensitive flows later in Q3.
- Sustained TPS: target 220 TPS during business hours (P50), with typical background traffic ~40 TPS off-peak.
- Burst profile: up to 650 TPS for short marketing-driven bursts; typically 3–6 minutes. We've seen up to 8 minutes once during a soft launch, so please size some headroom.
- Latency SLOs: target end-to-end p95 = 350ms. P99 goal less strict but should remain below 800ms for user-facing endpoints.
- Token mix (approx): average prompt 110 tokens, average completion 320 tokens. P95 combined length ~1200 tokens on some analytic generation jobs.
- Streaming: important for chunked responses in chat; we prefer streaming for UX but have some batched inference for internal batch scoring jobs.
- Preferred GPU: H100 preferred for p95 safety, but cost is a constraint — open to an A100-backed pool with tuned quantization if you can show comparable p95 for our token mix.
- Region: us-west-2 is preferred. We will need peering for Private later; will share VPC CIDR and security contact after internal review.

Attaching a quick capacity sketch (capacity_calcs_brightbridge.pdf) — it has sample traffic curves and a list of endpoints we expect to migrate to Dedicated in phase 1.

We can do Thu 22 Apr 10:00am PT for the deep-dive. Please invite the infra/bench team.

Sam
Sam Reed
Head of Platform Engineering | BrightBridge, Inc.
sam@brightbridge.ai | +1 (206) 555-4621

Attachments: capacity_calcs_brightbridge.pdf


From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sam Reed <sam@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 17:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity follow-up — GPU preference, 95th-pt TPS & token mix (next steps)

Perfect — thanks for the clear numbers, Sam. That gives us a good starting point. Quick notes and a proposed plan of action: 

Notes on sizing implications (quick intuition)
- Sustained 220 TPS with 650 TPS bursts and average ~430 tokens per request (prompt+completion) point toward a medium-to-large reserved pool. Exact GPU count depends heavily on kernel selection and whether you accept batching windows.
- H100 will give ~20–30% headroom on p95 for dense-generation at those lengths vs A100; quantization and fused-kernel runtimes can reduce the gap for many open models.
- Streaming requirement means we should favor micro-batching and lower batch latency targets for chat endpoints; we can still use larger batching for non-streamed scoring endpoints.

Proposed next steps
1) Deep-dive Thu 22 Apr 10:00am PT — calendar invite incoming (Ben will own the infra kickoff).
2) You (BrightBridge) — please upload the filled dedicated_tps_template.xlsx and the representative prompt set (anonymized) to the Drive link we shared. The template fields we especially need: p50/p95/p99 TPS, burst durations, token distributions per endpoint.
3) Redwood infra will run a small perf sweep (bench) on a representative model shape in us-west-2 once we have the prompt set. This will give us empirical p95 numbers for H100 vs A100 and guide GPU mix recommendations.
4) We'll return a recommended pool config (GPU mix, reserved count, burst policy, estimated monthly cost) within 3 business days after the bench runs.

Ben/Broad asks (Ben + Priyom)
- Ben: please prepare the test harness and the bench plan; we'll keep the first run constrained to a single AZ unless they explicitly want multi-AZ for fault tolerance.
- Priyom: please draft a short cost vs latency summary for H100 vs A100 using quantized variants (we can attach this to the proposal).

Sam — I’ll drop the calendar invite and also share a short checklist for the prompt set (tokenization, max lengths, any special tokens). If you can get the template back by EOD Fri 23 Apr we can start benching early next week.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: dedicated_tps_template.xlsx


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sam Reed <sam@brightbridge.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 18:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity follow-up — GPU preference, 95th-pt TPS & token mix (next steps)

Sam — invite received, looking forward to the deep-dive on Thu. Quick logistics so we come prepared:
- Please upload the anonymized prompt set and fill the template (or share a sample if you want us to pre-fill some rows). The bench requires at least 200 representative prompts to get stable p95 estimates.
- Confirm the specific endpoints (chat vs generation vs batch scoring) and the expected percentage split at launch (e.g., chat 60%, generation 30%, batch 10%).
- If you have a hard budget target per month, tell us now so Priyom can tailor the H100/A100 mix accordingly in the cost sheet.

We will bring a preliminary bench plan and a runbook for observing KV cache behavior and autoscale triggers. See you Thursday.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: bench_runbook_template.md (sent in calendar invite)

title: Re: Unexpected surge on recent invoice — investigating potential duplicate consumption
preview:
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@greenbranch.health>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Nguyen <sara.nguyen@greenbranch.health>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Unexpected surge on invoice 2027-03

Hi Ava —

Hope you had a nice weekend. We just reviewed our March billing and saw a very large, unexpected jump in token consumption dated 2027-03-14 02:00–06:00 UTC. The invoice line items show what appears to be the usual baseline for our chat usage, then a sudden block of ~1.8M additional tokens charged. That surge doesn't match activity on our side — we throttled sessions during that window.

Can you:
- Confirm whether these tokens are genuine (i.e., came from our calls) or if there was a counting/replay issue on Redwood's side.
- If there was an error, outline how you will correct it and timing for a credit.

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 11:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Unexpected surge on invoice 2027-03 — GreenBranch (root cause & recommendation)

Ava/Marissa/Hannah — initial findings from the infra side: we saw a transient request-id collision in our fronting queueing layer during a rolling deploy on 2027-03-14 02:08–02:35 UTC. Two behaviors combined: (1) a rare hash collision in our short-lived request dedupe table, and (2) a downstream retry loop where a subset of RPC retries were not marked as successful before dedupe window expired. That allowed roughly 1.7M tokens to be recorded twice for a small cohort of sessions.

What we did:
- Confirmed affected request IDs by correlating timestamps and the customer's sample slice; attached a trimmed debug export (redwood_root_cause_draft.txt).
- Fixed the dedupe table expiry bug (hotfix rolled 2027-03-15 04:20 UTC) so future requests are not at risk.

Estimate and proposal:
- Estimated duplicated billing: ~1.7M tokens (±8%), which maps to $4,200 at GreenBranch's committed rate.
- Recommendation: apply a one-time credit of $4,200 to their account for the March invoice, plus a brief postmortem that explains the root cause and the fix.
- For records, we propose to base the credit on the intersection slice Tom provided (greenbranch_usage_slice.csv) which matches our debug traces.

I recommend Marissa issue the credit note and Ava send the customer a short customer-facing note with the credit and the link to our postmortem.

Attachments: redwood_root_cause_draft.txt (text, 28KB)

title: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sana Roy <sana.roy@medicore.com>, Timothy Park <tpark@medicore.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options

Hi Sana / Tim —

Thanks again for a helpful call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we have alignment before we spin up the trial environment. I pulled these points from our conversation; please flag anything I missed.

Summary of goals (what success looks like for Medicore):
- Verify end-to-end latency for query-heavy workflows under 60ms p50 and acceptable p95 for peak windows.
- Validate model quality on your diagnostic prompt set: >= 92% pass rate on intent extraction and <5% hallucination on the clinical QA set.
- Confirm telemetry and audit log fidelity (token-level tracing + request IDs) for compliance review.
- Exercise failover path (hosted -> dedicated sandbox) under simulated capacity limits.

Trial scope & environment options discussed:
1) Quick path: Hosted API sandbox (Redwood-hosted) — fastest to get started, minimal lift; best for early quality and latency baselines.
2) Dedicated sandbox (reserved GPUs in our staging cluster) — predictable throughput for load testing; recommended if you want to validate throughput at scale.
3) VPC trial (Private) — for evaluation with your network controls and KMS; requires network onboarding and ~2–3 weeks to complete.

Recommended initial plan (we suggest a 3-week exploratory trial):
- Week 0 (this week): Kickoff, access + sample prompt exchange, share evaluation prompt set and success criteria.
- Week 1: Hosted baseline runs (quality + latency) — Redwood provides host endpoints and initial eval dashboard.
- Week 2: If throughput testing is needed, switch to Dedicated sandbox for peak/scale tests.
- Week 3: Optional VPC onboarding for security review (parallel preparatory work can start week 1).

Owners & immediate asks:
- Medicore: share your evaluation prompt sets (clinical QA + extraction), and a list of sensitive fields for masking (Sana / Tim).
- Redwood AE (Ava): coordinate kickoff, schedule demo for engineering team, manage billing/trial limits.
- Redwood Solutions (Ben): SOC2 artifacts, trial SOW and monitoring dashboard configuration.

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sana Roy <sana.roy@medicore.com>, Timothy Park <tpark@medicore.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options

Hi Sana / Tim —

Thanks again for a helpful call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we have alignment before we spin up the trial environment. I pulled these points from our conversation; please flag anything I missed.

Summary of goals (what success looks like for Medicore):
- Verify end-to-end latency for query-heavy workflows under 60ms p50 and acceptable p95 for peak windows.
- Validate model quality on your diagnostic prompt set: >= 92% pass rate on intent extraction and <5% hallucination on the clinical QA set.
- Confirm telemetry and audit log fidelity (token-level tracing + request IDs) for compliance review.
- Exercise failover path (hosted -> dedicated sandbox) under simulated capacity limits.

Trial scope & environment options discussed:
1) Quick path: Hosted API sandbox (Redwood-hosted) — fastest to get started, minimal lift; best for early quality and latency baselines.
2) Dedicated sandbox (reserved GPUs in our staging cluster) — predictable throughput for load testing; recommended if you want to validate throughput at scale.
3) VPC trial (Private) — for evaluation with your network controls and KMS; requires network onboarding and ~2–3 weeks to complete.

Recommended initial plan (we suggest a 3-week exploratory trial):
- Week 0 (this week): Kickoff, access + sample prompt exchange, share evaluation prompt set and success criteria.
- Week 1: Hosted baseline runs (quality + latency) — Redwood provides host endpoints and initial eval dashboard.
- Week 2: If throughput testing is needed, switch to Dedicated sandbox for peak/scale tests.
- Week 3: Optional VPC onboarding for security review (parallel preparatory work can start week 1).

Owners & immediate asks:
- Medicore: share your evaluation prompt sets (clinical QA + extraction), and a list of sensitive fields for masking (Sana / Tim).
- Redwood AE (Ava): coordinate kickoff, schedule demo for engineering team, manage billing/trial limits.
- Redwood Solutions (Ben): SOC2 artifacts, trial SOW and monitoring dashboard configuration.
- Redwood Ops (Marcus): provisioning Dedicated sandbox if requested; network checklist for VPC.

Attachments: I attached a first-pass Method of Procedure (Medicore_MoP_v1.pdf) and a trial SOW draft to accelerate procurement.

Proposed immediate next step: 30-minute kickoff on Tue 4/19 to exchange prompt sets and finalize which environment to start with — are you available 10:00–10:30am PT?

Thanks,

Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

title: Operator signal blip — customer readout and commitments
preview:
From: Jake Barnes <jake@starlark-pay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Ops Team <ops@starlark-pay.com>
Cc: Lauren Miles <lauren.miles@starlark-pay.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: brief interruption impacting verification calls

Hi Ben / Redwood team,

We saw a brief spike in failed verification calls between 08:42–08:51 PT this morning that caused ~0.9% of transactions to be retried by our gateway. Our SREs captured traces and we'd like a short readout today with a timeline and whether there are any systemic concerns. Can you share a short RCA and the actions you plan to take?

Thanks,
Jake
Jake Barnes
Head of Platform, Starlark Payments


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Jake Barnes <jake@starlark-pay.com>, Ops Team <ops@starlark-pay.com>, Lauren Miles <lauren.miles@starlark-pay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 11:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Operator signal blip — preliminary readout and attachments

Jake — thanks for the heads up and for the traces. Attaching a short preliminary RCA, an annotated timeline, and the mitigations checklist we intend to run. Summary below; full details in attachments.

Summary (TL;DR)
- What happened: An operator-fallback signal triggered during autoscaler handoff which caused a small set of inference workers to restart and drop short-lived sessions.
- Impact window: 2027-04-19T08:42:13 — 2027-04-19T08:51:02 PT.
- Customer-visible effect: ~0.9% additional verification retries for customers using sticky sessions during that window.
- Root cause (prelim): a race between autoscaler drain + operator-fallback logic where a health check false-negative caused premature eviction of sessions on three hosts.

Immediate actions we took
- Rolled back the aggressive fallback threshold at 08:53 PT.
- Rebalanced sessions and primed cache for affected pools by 09:05 PT.
- Added synthetic probes to the affected pool to detect recurrence (deployed 09:22 PT).

Next steps (by owner/ETA)
- Engineering (Hannah): Patch to avoid race condition in the drain handler and add stronger gating (ETA 2027-04-23).
- Platform (David): Add post-drain reconciliation job to re-associate in-flight sessions if host drains unexpectedly (ETA 2027-04-27).
- Customer comms (Ben/Marissa): Share final RCA and verification checklist; offer compensatory credits if you want them to be reviewed by billing (ETA 2027-04-20).

Attachments: operator-signal-blip-rca.pdf (pdf), timeline-annotated.csv (csv), mitigations-checklist.xlsx (xlsx)

We want to be transparent — attached is the annotated timeline and the rationale for our fixes. If you'd like, Marissa and I can run a 30-minute readout later today or tomorrow to walk through the timeline and answer questions.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0139
Redwood Inference


From: Lauren Miles <lauren.miles@starlark-pay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jake Barnes <jake@starlark-pay.com>, Ops Team <ops@starlark-pay.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 16:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Operator signal blip — preliminary readout and attachments

Ben / Marissa / Hannah — thanks for the quick turnaround and the attachments. A few follow-ups from our side:

1) Can you confirm whether any PII-bearing requests were retried or duplicated during the retries? Our compliance team needs confirmation for audit trails.
2) We want a runbook entry describing how we can detect this on our side and any recommended client-side mitigations.
3) We may request an account credit for the window; who is the right contact to kick that off?

If you can respond to (1) in writing that would be ideal for our auditors; we can set up a call to go over the technical detail after that.

-Lauren
Lauren Miles
VP, Engineering Operations, Starlark Payments


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Miles <lauren.miles@starlark-pay.com>, Jake Barnes <jake@starlark-pay.com>, Ops Team <ops@starlark-pay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Operator signal blip — preliminary readout and attachments

Lauren — quick answers inline, and follow-up plan below.

1) PII duplication/retries: We completed a trace-level sweep of the affected window. No bodies with PII were re-sent or duplicated; the retries were at the transport layer with idempotent verification requests (your gateway retried, and our logs show the request payloads were identical and not reprocessed by the verification backend). We will attach an audit excerpt to the Drive link for your compliance team.

2) Runbook entry: Hannah is preparing a short runbook snippet you can add to your ops playbooks (detect: increased 5xx ratio on verification endpoint plus 429 spike from sticky sessions; mitigate: fall back to non-sticky routing, and contact Redwood support to request pool rebalance). ETA for runbook doc: 2027-04-21.

3) Credits: Marissa will own the credit review. Marissa — can you confirm the next steps (scope, estimate, and billing contact) and we will open a billing ticket and share a DocuSign if needed.

Follow-up call: We're available tomorrow 09:30–10:00 PT for a short readout with engineering — I can send a calendar invite if that works.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

title: Scaling credit and target rate qualification
preview:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@novaops.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Scaling credit and target rate qualification

Hi Elena — thanks again for taking the call earlier today. Quick recap + suggested next steps so we can line up an actionable pricing proposal: 

Call highlights:
- Current pilot: inference for search re-ranking + embeddings for semantic search.
- Short-term volume: ~15M tokens/mo (mostly generation for search rerank).
- Growth profile: expect to ramp to ~60M tokens/mo within 6 months if model performance holds.
- Top priority: predictable unit cost and a runway to scale without sudden price jumps.

What we need from you to firm up a committed-use proposal:
1) Confirm the monthly token bands you can commit to over 12 and 24 months (best-effort is fine).
2) Target effective price you’re optimizing for (e.g., $/1M tokens) — you mentioned wanting a sub-$600 range on the call, pls confirm.
3) Procurement timeline and whether NovaOps would be able to sign a PO by end of Q2.

What we can offer and next steps we propose:
- Startup program: qualifying credits + introductory effective rates for the first 3 months to de-risk ramp.
- Committed-use discounts: tiered by annual token commitment; deeper discounts for multi-year deals.
- We’ll prepare 2 scenarios (12mo and 24mo) with explicit $/1M math and net-first-year cost.

I attached the redwood-demo-deck and will follow with a simple pricing spreadsheet so you can share with finance/procurement. If you can reply with the monthly ranges and target $/1M, I’ll push a tailored quote by EOD tomorrow.

Thanks — Jonas
Jonas Weber
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood-demo-deck.pdf (presentation, 12 slides)

From: Elena Rossi <elena@novaops.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: procure@novaops.ai
Date: 2027-04-19T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Scaling credit and target rate qualification

Jonas — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Volumes: current steady-state is ~15M tokens/mo. If we move search reranking fully to your model we expect a ramp schedule: 15M -> 30M (month 1), 45M (month 3), 60M (month 6). Our finance team modeled a conservative plan at 40M/mo for year 1.
2) Budget target: our internal target is ~ $500 / 1M tokens effective net for year-1 after any credits. We flagged sub-$600 as a stretch but ideal.
3) Term & procurement: procurement wants to see 12- vs 24-month options. We can sign a PO by June 30 assuming legal & security review go smoothly.

We also attached a usage snapshot (sample logs and projected burn) so you can use real numbers in the scenarios. 

Also: what does the startup program look like? Are credits handed up-front or applied as invoice credit? If we qualify, how does that stack with committed-use discounts?

Best,
Elena Rossi
Head of Product
NovaOps

Attachment: novaops-usage-snapshot.csv (sample usage by day, last 30d)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@novaops.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-19T13:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Scaling credit and target rate qualification

Elena — appreciate the quick details, this is great and gives us exactly what we need to model. A few concrete notes on how the programs stack and an initial set of scenarios.

How startup credits work:
- Qualifying startups receive a one-time onboarding credit (applied as an invoice credit) and an introductory effective rate for months 1–3. For deals that convert to committed-use, the onboarding credit reduces the first invoice(s) and we apply the committed discount from month 1 of the term.

Committed-use discount mechanics (high level):
- Discount is applied to per-token list prices based on annualized token commitment (tokens/year).
- Tiers (example):
  - Band A: 0–250M tokens/year -> baseline discount (5–10%)
  - Band B: 250–1,200M tokens/year -> mid discount (15–30%)
  - Band C: 1,200M+ tokens/year -> highest discount (30–45%)
- Multi-year: commit 24 months and we add a 5–10% term bonus on top of tier discount, or offer a rebate structure that pays out at year-end based on actual consumption vs commitment.

Initial scenarios (using NovaOps ramp and target $500/1M):
- Scenario 1 — 12mo commitment (annualized ~480M tokens/year based on conservative 40M/mo):
  - List unit price: $900 / 1M tokens (example list)
  - Mid-tier discount (25%) => $675 / 1M
  - Apply onboarding credit spread across first 3 months => effective year-1 $/1M ~= $620
  - Startup credit can reduce first-quarter cashflow by ~$20k (example)

- Scenario 2 — 24mo commitment (same year-1 consumption):
  - Mid-tier discount (25%) + term bonus (7%) => effective discount ~32% => $612 / 1M
  - Possible year-end rebate if consumption exceeds committed bands (improves effective rate further)

Notes: these are illustrative — we’ll run exact math in the spreadsheet attached (pricing-scenarios.xlsx) using your provided usage snapshot. We can also show a higher commitment plan if NovaOps is comfortable locking in a larger floor (e.g., 1,000M+/yr) which materially reduces $/1M.

Next steps I suggest:
1) Jonas will send the tailored workbook with the two scenarios filled in (includes gross $/1M, discount stack, credits, and net-first-year $/1M).
2) You share preferred term (12 vs 24) and any procurement constraints we should know about.
3) If positive, we introduce legal to get a term sheet + SOW and copy security for SOC2 evidence pack.

Attaching the scenarios workbook now. If you prefer, we can run a 30-min pricing review with finance next week to walk through the math live.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference

Attachment: pricing-scenarios.xlsx (12-tab workbook: assumptions, 12mo/24mo math, rebate models)

From: Elena Rossi <elena@novaops.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: procure@novaops.ai
Date: 2027-04-20T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Scaling credit and target rate qualification

Ben/Jonas — this is helpful and the scenarios look promising at a glance. Two quick asks before we route this to procurement:

1) Can you produce one consolidated quote that shows: 12mo vs 24mo net $/1M (year-1 effective), upfront/annual cashflow, and a line showing onboarding credit application? Procurement wants clear comparison columns.
2) Please include references to SOC2 and our required data residency language (we’re US-only for now). Legal will want to see how those constraints affect timeline/cost.

If you can get that over by EOD Thursday we’ll aim to review with procurement and come back with a preferred path. We’re leaning towards 12mo with the option to top-up to 24mo after quarter 2, but want to see the net math.

Appreciate the speed — Elena
Elena Rossi
Head of Product | NovaOps

Quoted attachments requested: consolidated quote (pdf), SOC2 audit summary (pdf)

If you can get that over by EOD Thursday we’ll aim to review with procurement and come back with a preferred path. We’re leaning towards 12mo with the option to top-up to 24mo after quarter 2, but want to see the net math.

Appreciate the speed — Elena
Elena Rossi
Head of Product | NovaOps

Quoted attachments requested: consolidated quote (pdf), SOC2 audit summary (pdf)

preview:

Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 09:12:45 -0700
Subject: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Hi Matt / Acme AP team —

Thanks for taking a look at our onboarding questions. We're finalizing vendor registration and need a quick confirmation on a few items so our billing team can start sending invoices without delays: 

- What exact PO fields must appear on invoice PDF for automatic matching in your ProcureVault workflow (PO number, PO line item ID, Bill-to legal name, company tax id)?
- Is the vendor registration portal the only acceptable place to submit tax documents (W-9 / VAT cert), or can we email a signed copy to accounts-payable?
- We plan to use a third-party remittance agent (Aurelia Payments) to collect funds on our behalf. Do you accept a Delegated Payment Authorization (signed letter) that designates Aurelia as the remit-to/payee on our vendor record, or do you require Redwood to have the vendor record with a remit-to subfield?
- If delegation is allowed, what proof do you need (sample invoice with remit-to, signed DPA, portal flag)?

I've attached a sample invoice template and our vendor legal name/tax ID for reference. If you prefer, happy to jump on a 20 minute call to walk through the fields.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0127
[Attachment: sample_invoice_template.pdf (pdf)]

From: Matt Rios <matt.rios@procurevault.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@acme-corp.com
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 10:05:30 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Ben —

Thanks for the clear list. Quick answers inline and attached enrollment guidance.

- Matching: We require PO number, vendor legal name, and vendor tax identifier to be present on the invoice PDF. Line-level matching requires the PO line ID and quantity/unit price when invoicing against specific lines.
- Tax docs: Complete vendor registration in the ProcureVault Supplier Portal is preferred so docs are linked to the vendor record. We will accept emailed W-9s or VAT certs temporarily but final enrollment must be through the portal.
- Delegated remittance: We support third-party remittance agents but need a signed Delegated Payment Authorization (DPA) on file that authorizes Aurelia to receive payment on behalf of Redwood. The DPA must be signed by an authorized Redwood officer and reference the Redwood vendor ID in our system.
- Proof needed: A signed DPA, a sample invoice indicating the remit-to address, and confirmation that the vendor portal lists Aurelia as the remit-to agent.

Attached: ProcureVault_vendor_enroll_guide.pdf; PO_mapping_template.xlsx

If you send the DPA as a PDF and the enrollment form, we can expedite and issue a vendor ID within 48 hours after verification.

Best,
Matt Rios
Senior Partnerships Manager | ProcureVault
matt.rios@procurevault.com
[Attachment: ProcureVault_vendor_enroll_guide.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: PO_mapping_template.xlsx (spreadsheet)]

Ben —

Legal did a quick pass on the delegate authorization draft. We can sign the DPA after two small edits: (1) add a clause that Redwood remains the invoice-originating party for tax and audit purposes; (2) include our EIN in the header and a limited liability statement for Aurelia as a collection agent.

Operational note: Creating a single vendor record for Redwood in the customer's portal and setting Aurelia as the remit-to agent is preferable — it preserves PO matching on Redwood's vendor ID while allowing payments to go to the agent. Creating separate vendor records tends to break PO auto-acceptance (many AP systems treat separate vendor names as distinct suppliers).

I've attached the redlined DPA draft. Marcus — can you confirm whether our billing system can produce a sample invoice with the remit-to fields as ProcureVault requested? If yes, we should include that sample when we submit the enrollment.

title: Sapphire AI — Post-call technical confirmation & next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@sapphire.ai>, Martin Cole <martin.cole@sapphire.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T14:12:00-07:00
Subject: Sapphire AI — quick post-call summary + asks

Hi Elena + Martin,

Thanks again for the demo earlier — good discussion. I wanted to capture the key pain points, confirm goals, and list the action items we discussed so we have a single reference before the technical follow-up. Please correct anything I get wrong.

Key pain points (what you told us):
- Current embed + rerank pipeline is slow at peak (tail latency spikes during bursts, affecting UX).
- Re-ranking cost is growing with query volume; you want predictable unit economics.
- Need structured output (JSON schema) from LLMs to feed downstream rules and metadata extraction reliably.
- Some compliance constraints: data residency for EU customers in future expansion (flagged).

Primary goals (next 90 days):
1) Proof point: run your rerank workload in a Redwood sandbox to measure latency and cost at 1M QPS-month equivalent.
2) Technical alignment: confirm SDK changes required for streaming + function-calling + structured outputs so your engineering team can prototype.
3) Rollout plan: mutual action plan for a pilot (benchmarks, success criteria, owners).

Technical notes we captured from call:
- You prefer a streaming-first integration to avoid client-side blocking; function-calling will be used to return structured metadata.
- Typical request sizes: ~120 tokens input, expected 40–80 tokens output.
- Expected initial throughput: ~100k queries/day (peaks to 250k/day) — attached is the CSV you mentioned for more detail.
- You currently run embedding + rerank in-house; plan is to offload inference to a hosted endpoint but with the option to move to dedicated capacity later.

Immediate asks from our side:
- Can you confirm the peak TPS numbers in the attached sapphire-expected-throughput.csv? (Martin)
- Please share a small sanitized sample of queries + rerank labels (or a synthetic sample) so Karthik can run a quick cost/latency sim.
- Confirm whether you need GDPR data residency for any of the pilot users (Elena).

Next steps (proposed):
- We’ll provision a Redwood sandbox API key and run a 1M-query/month simulation against a quantized model variant to estimate per-query cost. (Ben / Karthik)
- Technical deep-dive to go over SDK integration and function-calling (propose Wed Apr 21 10:00am PT).
- Draft mutual action plan + success criteria shared by EOD tomorrow.

Attachments: redwood-inference-integration-guide-v1.2.pdf; sapphire-expected-throughput.csv

Thanks — I’ll follow up with a calendar invite for the deep-dive.

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192
https://redwood.ai


From: Elena Park <elena.park@sapphire.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Martin Cole <martin.cole@sapphire.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Sapphire AI — quick post-call summary + asks

Thanks Ben — helpful summary. A couple quick clarifications from our side:

- Peak TPS: the CSV is accurate for peak daily volume; to be explicit: 3–4 TPS sustained with bursts to ~8 TPS during business hours, but our latency sensitivity is more about P95/P99 (those spikes cause user complaints).
- Cost question: can you ballpark token cost for 1M queries/month with the profile you noted (120 in / 60 out)? We understand this is estimate-level.
- Data: we’ll provide a sanitized sample (500 queries + labels) by end of day tomorrow. Attaching a quick architecture diagram so you can see how our rerank pipeline sits between the search index and the UI.

Couple of asks on our side:
- Confirm whether Redwood’s streaming + function-calling SDK can emit incremental partial JSON (we rely on partial results to render progressive UI).
- We’d like an optional dedicated capacity conversation later — but pilot should be hosted for speed to experiment.

Attaching: sapphire-ppg-architecture.png

Elena Park
Head of Product 
Sapphire AI
elena.park@sapphire.ai


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@sapphire.ai>, Martin Cole <martin.cole@sapphire.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-19T09:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Sapphire AI — quick post-call summary + asks

Elena — great, thanks for the clarifications and the diagram. A few technical confirmations and a proposed plan below.

Streaming + partial JSON: our streaming SDK supports partial token emission, and we can emit well-formed partial JSON chunks with an escape/merge strategy so the client can progressively parse. There is a small SDK change we recommend (two-line change in the JS stream parser) to support nested structured fields when using function-calling + streaming; Karthik can scope a short PR.

Preliminary cost note: assuming a quantized 4-bit variant we often see ~40–60% token cost reduction vs baseline. For a rough estimate: 1M queries/month with 180 tokens total (in+out) maps to ~180M tokens => ballpark tokenized cost X (we'll run the exact sim in the sandbox and share numbers). I avoided hard numbers until we run the sim.

Pilot proposal (short):
- Week 0: Redwood provisions sandbox API key + test environment. (owner: Ben)
- Week 1: Run 1M-query/month simulation with your sample/synthetic queries; send initial latency/cost report. (owner: Karthik)
- Week 2: Joint deep-dive to review results and SDK tweak (owner: Engineering)
- Week 3–4: Pilot rollout with small subset of real traffic + success criteria validation.

I’ve attached a draft mutual action plan (mutual-action-plan-draft.docx). Proposed deep-dive: Wed Apr 21 10:00am PT — does that work for your engineering team?

Action items assigned:
- Elena / Martin: share sanitized sample and confirm GDPR/residency needs.
- Karthik: run a short sim once sample is in and prepare a preliminary cost/latency runbook.
- Ben / Marissa: coordinate sandbox key and calendar invite.

Thanks — looping Karthik for technical scheduling.

—
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@sapphire.ai>, Martin Cole <martin.cole@sapphire.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-20T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Sapphire AI — quick post-call summary + asks

Elena / Martin — thanks for moving quickly on the sample. Confirming the deep-dive at Wed Apr 21 10:00am PT; I’ll send a calendar invite in the next 30 minutes that includes an agenda:

Agenda (30–45m):
- 10m: Overview of your rerank flow and success criteria (Sapphire)
- 15m: Karthik runs through integration approach (SDK changes, streaming + function calling pattern)
- 10m: Benchmarks we’ll run in the sandbox and what data we need
- 5–10m: Next steps / owners / timelines

What Redwood will provide before the call:
- Sandbox API key + example request templates (Ben)
- Short runbook for ingesting sanitized sample (Karthik)
- A baseline cost/latency simulation note after we run the sim (Ben / Karthik)

Please drop the sanitized sample to this thread or share a Drive link. If you prefer, we can accept a zipped CSV to: integrations@redwood.com (we’ll only use it for the sim).

Looking forward — if anything urgent, reach out to my cell +1 (650) 555-0244.

Marissa Cole
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

There is a small SDK change we recommend (two-line change in the JS stream parser) to support nested structured fields when using function-calling + streaming; Karthik can scope a short PR.

Preliminary cost note: assuming a quantized 4-bit variant we often see ~40–60% token cost reduction vs baseline. For a rough estimate: 1M queries/month with 180 tokens total (in+out) maps to ~180M tokens => ballpark tokenized cost X (we'll run the exact sim in the sandbox and share numbers). I avoided hard numbers until we run the sim.

Pilot proposal (short):
- Week 0: Redwood provisions sandbox API key + test environment. (owner: Ben)
- Week 1: Run 1M-query/month simulation with your sample/synthetic queries; send initial latency/cost report. (owner: Karthik)
- Week 2: Joint deep-dive to review results and SDK tweak (owner: Engineering)
- Week 3–4: Pilot rollout with small subset of real traffic + success criteria validation.

I’ve attached a draft mutual action plan (mutual-action-plan-draft.docx). Proposed deep-dive: Wed Apr 21 10:00am PT — does that work for your engineering team?

Action items assigned:
- Elena / Martin: share sanitized sample and confirm GDPR/residency needs.
- Karthik: run a short sim once sample is in and prepare a preliminary cost/latency runbook.
- Ben / Marissa: coordinate sandbox key and calendar invite.

Thanks — looping Karthik for technical scheduling.

—
Ben Carter
